Themes: Arjuna's Dilemma, Duty, Compassion
 
URL: https://youtu.be/h8hMfQp6ZFs?t=0
 
Content-type: story
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: OM NAMO BHAGAVATE VASUDEVAYA. Reading from Srimad Bhagavad Gita, Chapter 1, entitled, Observing the Army on the Battlefield of Kuruksetra, Verse No. 45: YADIMAM APRATITARAM ASASTRAM SASTRA PANAYAH DATARASTRA RANIHANYOS TANME SHEMATARAM BHAVET. Translation and Purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada: I would consider it better for the sons of Dhritarashtra to kill me unarmed and unresisting rather than fight with them. Purport by His Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada: It is a custom according to Kshatriya fighting principles that unarmed and unwilling foe should not be attacked. Arjuna, however, in such an enigmatic position, decided he would not fight if he were attacked. He did not consider how much the other party was bent upon fighting. All these symptoms are due to soft-heartedness resulting from his being a great devotee of the Lord. Verse No. 46. I will read myself. Sanjaya uvacha evam uttvarjunasankhe rathopastupa visat visrajya sasaramchapam sotra samvitnam anasah. Translation: Sanjaya said, Arjuna, having thus fought on the battlefield, tossed aside his bow and arrows and sat down on the chariot, his mind overwhelmed with grief. Perfect. While observing the situation of his enemy, Arjuna stood up on the chariot, but he was so afflicted, he pleaded with lamentation that he sat down again, setting aside his bow and arrows. Such a trained and soft-hearted person in the devotional service of the Lord is fit to receive self-knowledge. I would consider it better for the sons of Dhritarashtra to kill me unarmed and unresisting rather than fight with them. Sanjaya said, Arjuna, having thus spoken on the battlefield, cast aside his bow and arrows and sat down on the chariot, his mind overwhelmed with grief.

Content: yadimam apratikaram asastram sastra-pāṇaya. It is the custom between the kṣatriyas, that in the fighting, if the other party hasn't got weapon to fight, this party will supply him weapon. Note that the other party without weapon and this party will take opportunity to kill him. This is not the rules and regulation of fighting. There are many rules and regulations of the fighting. Not that because he's my enemy, I shall kill him anyway. No. There are rules and regulations. If the enemy has broken his chariot, he's fallen down on the ground, he's been killed. The other party also will immediately get down from the chariot. Suppose he is fleeing, then if one is on the foot and another on the chariot, so he will be in superior position. Therefore the opposite party must also get down from the chariot and walk with him. That means the defeated enemy should be given all opportunities. So he may not think that unnecessarily, in weak position, he has been killed. No. So Arjuna says… Arjuna, of course, did later on. When he killed Karna, Karna was without weapon, and he fell down from the chariot. Karna, after falling down from the chariot, and he was trying to raise the wheel of the chariot from the mud. So Kṛṣṇa asked Arjuna that this is the opportunity to kill him. You kill him immediately. So at that time he had no weapons, and he was trying to kill Karna, and he fell down from the chariot. And in that position he was killed by Arjuna, by the order of Kṛṣṇa. This is violating the laws. So you can violate the laws on the order of Kṛṣṇa. You cannot do. This is surrender.

Then, nārtyasya-cāgadha The medicine, a person is suffering from some disease, some fatal disease. Because he has got money, he can employ first class physician, first class medicine, and simply by engaging such things, counteracting, pratikāraṁ. He cannot be safe. There are many examples like that. So the conclusion is, tāvat vibhūtaṁ vṛtāṁ tada pe kṣitānāṁ If you neglect, if you have decided, that this person, this child cannot be safe, then any amount of pratikāraṁ will not help. Will not help. Therefore, real pratikāraṁ is Kṛṣṇa. Real pratikāraṁ is Kṛṣṇa. Counter it. So, better we take shelter of Kṛṣṇa. As Kṛṣṇa says, sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vrajaḥ. You must always know that no counteraction will be beneficial for us unless we are protected by Kṛṣṇa. So therefore, our business is fully surrender to Kṛṣṇa and seek His protection. Kṛṣṇa says, yes, ahaṁ tvā sarva-pāpebhaṁ akṣuṣyaṁ, I shall give you protection. This is Bhagavān's Bhagavad-gītā philosophy. We have to believe on the assertion of Kṛṣṇa that He is prepared to give me all protection provided I have fully surrendered unto His lotus feet. Thank you very much.

Themes: renunciation, devotion, surrender
 
URL: https://youtu.be/jQgypRuR8Bw?t=148
 
Content-type: story
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: Despite repeated attempts to leave home for Jagannath Puri to accompany the Lord, he was sent back. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu advised him against false renunciation, Markata Vairagya, urging him to engage in befitting renunciation, Yukta Vairagya. Raghunath Das married as per his parents' wish but remained internally devoted to Lord Chaitanya. Raghunath Das harbored a deep longing to reunite with Lord Gauranga. Ceasing an opportunity to escape, he fled to Puri, surrendering to Lord Chaitanya. In Puri, he exhibited exemplary renunciation, pleasing Mahaprabhu who gifted him Govardhanashila and Gunjamala. Raghunath Das' life of austerity and devotion remains eternal. He is an enduring source of inspiration, epitomizing the essence of surrender and devotion to the Lord. While most cannot imitate his extreme renunciation, his example encourages seekers to cultivate deeper spiritual dedication and humility in their own lives.

URL: https://youtu.be/3AxaWNAZbWU?t=0
 
Content-type: philosophy
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. In Srimad Bhagavate Gita, Chapter 2, Contained Bhagavate Gita, Summarized, Verse 1, Sanjayo Vacha. Tam Tatha krupaya vishtam, ashrupurna kulekshanam, vishidantam idam vakyam, vacha Madhusudana. Translation in Perpetual Bhai's Divangresh. Translation: Sanjayo said, seeing Arjuna full of compassion and very sorrowful, his eyes brimming with tears, Madhusudana, Krishna, spoke the following words. Perpetual compassion, lamentation, and tears are all signs of ignorance of the real self. Compassion for the eternal soul is real self-realization. The word Madhusudana is significant in this verse. Lord Krishna killed the demon Madhu, and now Arjuna wanted Krishna to kill the demon of misunderstanding that had overtaken him in the discharge of his duty. No one knows where compassion should be applied. Compassion for the dress of a drowning man is senseless. A man fallen in the ocean of nescience cannot be saved simply by rescuing his outward dress, the gross material body. One who does not know this and laments for the outward dress is called a Shudra, or one who laments unnecessarily. Arjuna was a Kshatriya, and this conduct was not expected from him. Lord Krishna, however, can dissipate the lamentation of the ignorant man, and for this purpose the Bhagavad Gita was sung by him. This chapter instructs us in self-realization by an analytical study of the material body and the spirit soul. As explained by the supreme authority, Lord Shri Krishna, this realization is made possible by working with the fruity being situated in the fixed conception of the real self. Thus ends the Bhaktivinoda Purport.

Anyāvilāśitā sunyam jñāna-karmāda-anāvṛtaṁ ānukulyena kṛṣṇaṇu-śrīlanam bhakti-uttamāṁ. Uttamāṁ bhakti, first-class bhakti. What is that? Anyāvilāśitā sunyam. Without any kind of desire than to serve the Lord.

It is very difficult to realize कृष्ण consciousness. But there is very shortcut way recommended. Simply by chanting हरे कृष्ण हरे कृष्ण कृष्ण कृष्ण हरे हरे। You become uncontaminated of this material contamination. And thus being free from material contamination, you become eligible to go back to home, back to God. Thank you very much.
Themes: biography, renunciation, parampara
 
URL: https://youtu.be/6zqIuMP19VM?t=56
 
Content-type: biography
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: Today is the most auspicious disappearance day anniversary of one of the previous acharyas in our Sampradaya, His Divine Grace Srila Gaur Kishodas Babaji Maharaj. So a brief biography of Srila Gaur Kishodas: let us hear, and by his blessings, we will be able to advance in our Krishna consciousness by trying to follow some of the instructions that he has very nicely given us. Srila Gaur Kishodas Babaji Maharaj appeared sometime in the 1830s in the village of Vagyana in East Bengal, or Vagyana in East Bengal, which is what is now known as Bangladesh. During his boyhood, his mother and father arranged a marriage for him, and he remained in household affairs for more than 20 years. While a householder, he worked as a broker in the grain business, but even at that time, he exhibited his spirituality. After the demise of his wife, Srila Gaur Kishodas Babaji Maharaj left the country, left his business, and went to Vrindavan. There he approached Srila Bhagavad Das Babaji, one of the foremost disciples of Srila Jagannath Das Babaji Maharaj, and accepted from Bhagavad Das Babaji Maharaj the traditional Paramahamsa Vaishnava Babaji dress. After this, he became completely renounced and would stay under trees in various parts of the forest of the 168-mile Vraja Mandala or Vrindavan area. He continuously acted thus for a period of approximately 30 years. During this time, he would sometimes travel to the holy places of pilgrimage in northern and western India. Srila Gaur Kishodas Babaji Maharaj became very famous among the great devotees of Vrindavan and was awarded the appropriate title of Bhajananandi. Bhajananandi means one who is dedicated to remain in one place and chant the holy name of the Supreme Lord and completely absorb themselves in the chanting and hearing of the holy name of the Supreme Lord, but we should not try to imitate such great devotees. Rather we should accept the instructions of our spiritual master and try to follow those instructions in our practical life, while hearing the great qualities of our spiritual master and the previous Acharyas and their great deeds of devotion and service dedicated entirely for the pleasure of the Supreme Lord. Even though he received such honor, he never endeavored for even a small bit of material sense gratification and totally disapproved of the secret material desires of pseudo-renunciants and so many people who try to imitate great devotees, and prematurely they try to renounce this world, but it is not very easy to maintain the standard of renunciation exhibited by such paramahamsas as Sri Gaur Kishodas Babaji Maharaj.
Themes: renunciation, bhakti, parampara
 
URL: https://youtu.be/6zqIuMP19VM?t=301
 
Content-type: philosophy
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: He would perform his pure unalloyed devotional pastimes alone in a profound devotional mood. In the year 1897, during the month of Falgunna, when he was in his sixties, Srila Gaur Kishodas Babaji Maharaj proceeded to Navadvipa Dham from Vrindavan Dham, and he did this under the instruction of Srila Jagannath Das Babaji Maharaj. From this time until his disappearance, Srila Gaur Kishodas Babaji Maharaj remained in Navadvipa Dham and in different villages. He was visiting the various places of pastimes of Lord Gauranga, and he was realizing the non-difference between Navadvipa Dham and Vrindavan Dham. He begged by gathering dry foodstuff from the householders of the holy Dham. He would sometimes offer these foodstuffs to the Supreme Personality of Godhead by simply holding them in his hand. He was totally renounced, so he didn't have any elaborate arrangements for offering or cooking or anything like that. In order to cook, he would collect dry wood from the footpath, and he would wash earthen pots discarded by the villagers along the roadside near the Ganges River on the occasion of eclipses. These are considered totally impure, but a renunciant has nothing to do with purity and impurity of this world. Just he would take something of no value to others, and somehow, with those bare minimum things, he would maintain himself. This would actually ensure that such great devotees are never entangled in any material endeavors. They save time, maximum amount of time, for their spiritual activities exclusively. In order to clothe himself, he would go to the shore of the Ganges and collect and wash discarded cloth that had been used to cover dead bodies at the burning heart. This was his level of renunciation. Thus he always remained independent of the support of others simply by utilizing items of no value to others. In such a manner, he obtained the practical necessities of life. In 1897, Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur constructed his place of bhajana on the bank of the Saraswati River at Godurma Dvipa, one of the nine islands in Navadvipa, called Svananda Sukhada Kunja. This is the name Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur gave to that building he constructed. This was a gathering place for exalted souls, for great devotees. Here, Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur recited and explained the Srimad Bhagavatam and other topics regarding the conclusion of devotional service, and Srila Gaur Kishodas Babaji Maharaj would come and hear these talks with great delight. In this way, Srila Gaur Kishodas Babaji Maharaj accepted Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur as his shiksha guru. Also, Srila Gaur Kishodas Babaji Maharaj accepted Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Thakur as his only disciple. That's how you see in our parampara, His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivinoda Swami Prabhupada, the founder Acharya of ISKCON. His guru is Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Thakur. His guru is Srila Gaur Kishodas Babaji Maharaj, and his shiksha guru is Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur. This is the parampara in which Srila Prabhupada, the founder Acharya of ISKCON, is coming. So Srila Gaur Kishodas Babaji Maharaj is the spiritual master of the spiritual master of Srila Prabhupada.
Themes: renunciation, devotion, purity
 
URL: https://youtu.be/6zqIuMP19VM?t=574
 
Content-type: story
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: His only possessions, if at all you may consider them as possessions, were the Tulsi beads he wore around his neck, this Kanti Mala, and the Tulsi Mala he kept in his hand for counting his rounds. His Kanti Mala and his Japa Mala were his only two possessions. He also kept a few books, such as Narottam Das Thakur's Prarthana and Prema Bhakti Chandrika. These are two famous books, books written by Narottam Das Thakur, containing a collection of the songs he has composed, explaining the pure devotional service. Even though it appeared that Gaur Kishodas Babaji Maharaj was not a learned Sanskrit scholar, still the purport and conclusions of all scriptures were luminous in his heart and character. It is described in the scriptures that chanting of the holy name reveals all transcendental knowledge within the heart, and this is practically seen in the life of Srila Gaur Kishodas Babaji Maharaj. He knew all the conclusions of the scriptures without studying them separately. That is because his chanting was pure. We should not imitate this. It was never possible for anyone to serve him, for he was never willing to accept any kind of service from any person. He exhibited the highest standard of devotion to the Supreme Lord Sri Krishna, and because of his exemplary character, he was recognized as the personification of the deepest degree of separation from Lord Sri Krishna, Vipralamba Seva, service in separation from Krishna. This is what is taught by Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, especially in our Gaudiya Vaishnava Sampradaya, because in separation from Krishna, one feels intense mood of service to Krishna, not as much as when one is in the presence of Krishna. It is more intense than in the presence of Krishna. In separation from Krishna, one is able to serve with more intensity Krishna with that intense feeling of devotion towards Krishna. There are many incidents recorded by biographers about his preaching. Even though he is called a bhajananandi, he would not travel and actively preach, but anybody approached him for getting guidance, for getting some practical instructions, he was ever willing to give them his divine association. So I will just mention one or two such incidents which are very instructive for all of us. Once a lawyer by name Bhattacharya brought several sweets and prayerfully requested Srila Gautishwada's Babaji Maharaj to accept his offering. Srila Babaji Maharaj told him, "I never take any sweets." So then Bhattacharya replied, "There is nothing higher than accepting food that the Lord has directly accepted, indicating thereby that it is Prasadam. Why are you refusing Prasadam?" So then Srila Babaji Maharaj, Gautishwada's Babaji Maharaj, sharply retorted, "Whoever eats fish is like a prostitute." These are harsh words, but then they are required to cut through the several layers of illusion that is clouding the consciousness of our condition. So if someone has perverted desires and offers foodstuffs to Lord Chaitanya, these preparations can never be accepted as the Prasad of the Supreme Lord because the Lord does not partake of them. What does Krishna say in the Bhagavad Gita? Yome Bhaktya Prayacchati Patram Pushpam Phalam Toyam Yome Bhaktya Prayacchati. The important aspect of the offering, more than the item itself, is the devotion with which that offering is made. So Krishna, what does he want when we offer? He wants devotion. He wants Bhakti. So somebody is having lusty desires in his heart and then makes a show of offering, that is not accepted by Krishna. To offer foodstuffs to the Supreme Lord that have been contaminated by one's lusty desire should be considered a great offense. That means it is against our devotional interest. If one accepts the grains of a sinful person, then his mind becomes impure. This is very important for all of us, that why do we say eat only Prasadam, don't eat food that is not offered to Krishna, because we do not know sometimes the source of such food could be, if it is not properly offered to Krishna, then it could be from the sinful person, and then that contaminates our mind, so we should be very careful not to accept anything which is not Prasadam properly offered to Krishna. Thus one is directed by Babaji Maharaj not to accept food cooked by lustful persons, especially by those who know the actual standard of behavior expected of devotees of Sri Krishna, but who nevertheless indulge knowingly in illicit activities. So this is very, very important for us to remember, knowingly one should never indulge in sinful activities at the same time perform devotional service, especially offering of food stuff to the Supreme Lord.
Themes: association, devotion, illusion
 
URL: https://youtu.be/6zqIuMP19VM?t=976
 
Content-type: story
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu instructed Srila Swaroop Damodar Goswami as follows: when one eats food offered by a materialistic man, one's mind becomes contaminated, and when the mind becomes contaminated, one is unable to think of Krishna properly. A contaminated mind is not conducive for any form of devotional service. Our practice becomes obstructed. Srila Gaukishodas Babaji Maharaj always gave beneficial instructions to persons who approached him with faith and humility, no matter what their spiritual status might have been. On one such occasion, a seeker went to Babaji Maharaj to hear topics concerning the Supreme Lord, but he heard some harsh words from him instead. The seeker therefore resolved never again to go near Babaji Maharaj because he was feeling hurt by the harsh words spoken by Gaukishodas Babaji Maharaj. After some time, however, when he was very much distressed in his mind, he decided to go and see Babaji Maharaj. So Srila Gaukishodas Babaji Maharaj could immediately understand the reason for the depression of this particular seeker, and he told him, "You have left the association of devotees where topics about Krishna are discussed, and now you are living in a secluded place performing solitary worship of Krishna. When one hears the topic of Supreme Lord in the company of devotees, then one becomes freed from Maya's net of illusion, but if someone performs worship of the Lord in a secluded place, not in the association of devotees, one becomes caught by the material illusory energy of the Lord. As a consequence, instead of topics about the Supreme Lord possessing the heart, mundane subject matters will occupy the heart." The seeker replied, "I thought it better to reside in a secluded place and perform my own individual worship than to become disturbed by hearing the sharp words of a sadhu." He thought if I remain with a sadhu and the sadhu speaks very harsh words, then my mind will always be disturbed. I can peacefully go to some other place and do my own worship and be nicely situated to do devotional service. So Babaji Maharaj replied, "Please take note that these sadhus who speak sharp words to drive away the witch of the illusory energy are actually the only real devotees of Krishna and friends of the living entities. When a devotee of the Lord, who is always desirous of the living entity's ultimate welfare, chastises him just once with instructions meant to drive away maya, then that living being immediately makes plans to leave the saintly persons for his entire life. If you actually want to perform devotional service properly, then you must accept the harsh language of the sadhu as the medicine by which maya can be given up. It's just like when a doctor prescribes a medicine, even if it is bitter, you take it because you know that only by taking the proper medicine, even if it is bitter, it will cure you of your ailment or disease, so you should not look for something that is tasting sweet when it comes to taking medicine. Similarly, the harsh words of a sadhu may sound like very, very hurting, but actually they are meant simply to pull you out of illusion, which is the purpose of your doing devotional service. By this one can obtain the necessary spiritual advancement to successfully chant the holy name of the supreme lord."

Content: Then another instance, one other person, he inquired from Srila Gaurakeshwara's Babaji Maharaj as follows: he said, "We see that there are many Vaishnavas living with their wives who perform devotional service to Lord Hari. Will they get any benefit from such service?" Srila Babaji Maharaj replied, "The living entity is the eternal servant of Lord Krishna, but when the conditioned soul looks upon his wife and son simply by seeing their material external forms, then one does not have the eye of devotion, and he can never perceive his real identity as the eternal servant of Krishna. The conditioned souls always have inclination to enjoy their wives and children, that's natural, and they don't like to hear so much the topics of the supreme lord or to get association of the lord's devotees. If one does not attain the spiritual energy given by chanting the holy name, then one will become attracted by his wife and children. Then again, there are other persons who externally want to detach themselves from their wives and children and sense enjoyment, but they don't have any kind of external renunciation without actually having that detachment within the heart is like the renunciation of monkeys, markata vairagya, this is what it's called, and although they externally live away from their possessions, but their heart is dirty, the heart is not clean. So what is the characteristic of a monkey? A monkey lives naked, so it doesn't even have a piece of cloth, it doesn't have a proper house, it lives simply on the ground, it lives on the ground. So the monkey has the desire to enjoy so many female monkeys, not just one. Every male monkey will have so many male monkeys as a devotee or an ancient should reflect the inner detachment or should at least aim it also simultaneously strenuously trying for detachment from within the heart. The genuine vaishnava will never maintain an attitude of sense gratification towards his wife and children, but he will always regard himself. Those actually desirous of performing Haribhajana but in whose heart the weeds of material desires grow and were unable to give up excessive attachment of their wife and children should always try to hear from pure devotees and chant Hare Krishna and perform congregational chanting. Instead of leaving their family and going away to a secluded place, one should actually engage in such activities even while remaining in family life. In this way they can quickly become detached from the inclination to enjoy materially. Then they will gradually realize that if one completely surrenders to Krishna, then one will attain all the qualities of life. This is called auspiciousness. As long as one remains in bodily consciousness, one cannot obtain the mercy of Krishna.

That day at sunrise, his disciple Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Thakur left Sri Mayapur for the place where Srila Bhaktisiddhanta, sorry, Gaur Kishore das Babaji Maharaj had been staying in a place at Kuliyam. The head priest of Navadvipa's various temples and ashrams began to argue where would Babaji Maharaj's samadhi be made, the actual place. So there was a heated argument among so many different people. So because there was a material motive, whoever got the opportunity to prepare the samadhi of this great Paramahamsa, Srila Gaur Kishore das Babaji Maharaj, they would have huge number of people visit their temple and get handsomely from the pilgrims. So with this material motive, there was so much of argument. So Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Thakur could understand their material motive and he wanted to actually stop them from simply creating a controversy. Now, not only Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Thakur wanted to avoid the controversy, but even the Navadvipa's inspector of police, whose name was Jathindra Sinha at that time, he arrived at that place apprehending some breach of peace. So after much discussions, the other Vaishnavas who were running different ashrams and mats, they stated their position to the inspector. That Saraswati Thakur is not a sannyasi. He has no right to prepare a samadhi for a person who is completely renounced. Hearing their pronouncement, Saraswati Thakur replied in a voice like thunder, "I am the sole disciple of Paramahamsa Srila Gaurakeshwara das Babaji Maharaj. Even though I have not accepted sannyas, I am a celibate brahmachari and by the grace of Babaji Maharaj, I am not hypocritical, renunciant, addicted to abominable habits." So he put a challenge for those people who within the last one year or six months or three months or just even one month or even three days has not indulged in illicit association with women will be able to touch the spiritually blissful body of Srila Gaurakeshwara das Babaji Maharaj. If anyone else touches the body, he will be completely ruined. Hearing these words, the inspector of police asked Srila Saraswati Thakur, "How will evidence for this be produced?" So Saraswati Thakur replied, "I will believe whatever they say. Let them just tell it. That's all." After this challenge is offered, all the other Babaji's, they turned their backs and went away one by one. That means they all accepted that they were not leading a pure life. The inspector was dumbfounded. Then, Srila Bhaktisiddhan Saraswati Thakur actually prepared a nice samadhi and laid to rest the blissful transcendental body of Srila Gaurakeshwara das Babaji Maharaj. So let us end this presentation with the prayer to Srila Gaurakeshwara das Babaji Maharaj. Namo Gaurakeshwaraya Sakshad Vairagya Murtaye Vipralambhara Sambhode Padambhu Jayate Namaha. Themes: lineage, incarnation, devotion
 
URL: https://youtu.be/sDOmqVy2GoY?t=0
 
Content-type: story
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: Jiva Goswami has explained the genealogical history of Sri Rupa Goswami's family. They were the descendants of Sarvagya Satguru, a great scholar of the Vedas. He was an expert in Yajur Veda. Sri Sarvagya was a Brahmin and also one of the kings of Karnataka in the 12th century Shakabdha. In his lineage appeared a great devotee named Kumara Deva who had settled in Jaishur, Bangladesh. He got married to a very chaste lady named Revati Devi. In the course of time, Kumara Deva and Revati Devi gave birth to many sons. Most illustrious among them were Amara, later known as Sanatana Goswami, Santosha, known as Rupa Goswami and Vallabha, known as Anupama. It is believed that Sanatana was born around 1488 AD, while his younger brother Rupa appeared in this world around 1493 AD. It is mentioned in the Adi Leela of Sri Chaitanya Siddhartha Amritha that Sri Rupa, Sanatana and Anupama are the 43rd, 44th and 45th branches of Mahaprabhu's Desire Tree of devotion service and that they are the best of all. Anupama Vallabha Sri Rupa Sanatana etina shakha vrkshera paschime sarvottama. Sri Kavikarnapura reveals in Gauraganodesha Deepika that Sri Rupa Goswami was none other than the incarnation of Sri Rupa Manjari, one of the chief assistants of Lalitha Sakhi, one of the eight principal gopis of Vrindavan. Sri Sanatana Goswami was the combined incarnation of Rati Manjari and Lavanga Manjari and Sanatana Kumara. Jiva Goswami, the son of Anupama, was the incarnation of Ilaasa Manjari. After Kumara Deva had died, these three brothers came over and settled here at Malda, where they continued with their studies. As they grew up, they became exceptionally attractive to all of the society. They possessed beautiful bodily features, noble character and natural compassion towards every living being. They were embodiments of the Lord's love. These three brothers studied the science of logic, Nyaya, and the knowledge of the divine. They were also the descendants of Madhusudana Vachaspati, who was the brother of Sarvabhauma Bhattacharya. They also became proficient in Sanskrit, Arabic and Persian. When Sanatana Goswami was very young, he saw a dream in which a Brahmin gave him a copy of Srimad Bhagavatam. The next day, to his surprise, he indeed received Srimad Bhagavatam. From then on, Shri Sanatana began scrutinizingly studying Srimad Bhagavatam, leaving aside all other scriptures. Bengal, during that time, was under the Mughal rule of Nawab Hussain Shah. He is the same king who had employed Shri Mukunda Dasa of Shrikhanda as his personal doctor. Seeing the popularity of Shri Rupa and Sanatana amongst the great general populace, the Nawab decided to employ them as his ministers. In this way, he planned to bring the entire Hindu population under his control. If Rupa and Sanatana joined him, then his subjects would become more submissive, he thought. On the other hand, he notified Shri Rupa and Sanatana, telling them that if they became the ministers, he would award the Vaishnavas and Hindus with all freedom. Instead, if they did not accept his proposal, then he would torture and prosecute the masses. Hearing the king's warning, Shri Rupa, Sanatana and Anupama reluctantly agreed to serve the king. Their service was so exemplary that Shri Sanatana Goswami was made the Prime Minister of Bengal and Rupa Goswami was made the Home Minister. Due to their close proximity with the Muslims, the two brothers were excommunicated from the Hindu society by the orthodox caste Brahmins of Gaur Mandala. Thus, Shri Rupa Goswami came to be known as Dabarkhas and Shri Sanatana as Sakaramalik. The king grew very fond of them and considered them his own brothers. He awarded them with tremendous amounts of wealth and power. They settled down here at Ramkeli in Malda, where they constructed beautiful opulent palaces and gardens that served as a replica of Vruddhavan. Being pure devotees, Rupa and Sanatana always lived in the mood of separation from the Lord and his abode. Here at Ramkeli, the beautiful temples and ponds dug by our Goswamis are still present.
Themes: divine encounter, devotion, mercy
 
URL: https://youtu.be/sDOmqVy2GoY?t=350
 
Content-type: story
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: The beautiful deity of Shri Madana Mohana that was installed by them over here continues to accept our humble services till this day. They held the highest post in the government as they were very rich, yet the Goswamis did not possess even a grain of pride. People, scholars, Vaishnavas from all parts of the country would come to meet them and they would worship them with most intimate hospitality. Their humility and simplicity captured people's heart. There was a beautiful piece of architecture that we came across while visiting. This marvelous victory tower known as Firoz Minar is more than 26 meters in height. According to reliable sources, this tower was engineered by Sanatan Goswami while he served as Bengal Gov. Lord Chaitanya, during his second attempt to reach Vruddhavan, decided to take a roundabout route. Actually, his desire was not travelling to Vruddhavan, but instead he wanted to meet and shower his mercy upon Sri Rupa and Sanatana. Thousands of men accompanied the Lord during his journey. All of them were surcharged with deep ecstatic feelings, completely devoid of external consciousness and surrendering themselves completely. They sang and danced along. An ocean of love flooded the people with waves after waves of ecstasy. As the procession reached new places, more people joined. Wherever the Lord touched the ground with his lotus feet, people immediately gathered to take the dust. The people took out so much dirt that holes were created in the ground. Just by seeing the smiling face of the Lord, all their lamentations and unhappinesses became vanquished. Eventually, the Lord arrived at Ramkeli, where innumerable people arrived to take his darshan. Performing Sankirtana over here, the Lord danced and sometimes lost his consciousness out of intense love of God. When Nawab Hussain Shah came to know that Lord Chaitanya was attracting so much attention, he was astonished. His spies informed him how Mahaprabhu had immersed everyone in a wave of transcendental ecstasy. He learned how the people, irrespective of their caste, creed or religion, were raising their hands and engaging in the congregational chanting of Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. So the Nawab then asked Keshava Chhatri whether he knew anything about Lord Chaitanya. Keshava, in spite of knowing everything, tried to diplomatically downplay the situation. He replied that Chaitanya Deva was simply a touring mendicant and that only a few people had come to see him. He warned the King against considering the advice of his spies too seriously. After pacifying the King in this way, Keshava sent a Brahmana messenger to Lord Chaitanya, requesting him to leave without delay. The King then inquired from Rupa Goswami Dabarkas and asked his opinion. Rupa Goswami glorified Sri Chaitanya and revealed that he was none other than the Supreme Personality of God and Himself. He added that it was the Nawab's great fortune that the Lord has chosen to descend and manifest his pastime in this country. It was only by Mahaprabhu's mercy that the Nawab was enlightened. The Nawab was successful and victorious in all his affairs.
Themes: humility, surrender, divine guidance
 
URL: https://youtu.be/sDOmqVy2GoY?t=594
 
Content-type: story
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: Sri Rupa and Sanatana then began considering how they could meet Lord Chaitanya incognito. Thus, in the dead of the night, the two brothers went to meet the Lord. First, they met Nityananda Prabhu and Haridas Dhaka who introduced them to Sri Gaurahari. Out of great humility, the two brothers held straw between their teeth and fell down like a rod before Mahaprabhu. They were so overwhelmed that they began crying in joy. They introduced themselves as the lowest of men who were employed in the worst kind of employment. They glorified the Lord as Patitapavana, the deliverer of the fallen, and assured him that none in the world qualified as being more fallen than them. Out of sheer humility, they declared themselves millions of times more fallen than even Jagai and Madai, the sinful brothers whom Lord Chaitanya had previously delivered. Jagai and Madai are millions of times more virtuous. Adhamma and Patita are sinners. We are two people. Thus, they tried to establish themselves as the befitting candidates upon which Lord Chaitanya should shower his costless mercy. Hearing their prayers, Lord Chaitanya became very pleased and blessed them wholeheartedly. He then revealed that the two brothers were his eternal servants. Mahaprabhu also informed them that he had received several of their letters, which reflected their humble state of mind and consciousness. He accepted them and awarded them the name Rupa unto Dhabi's caste and Sanatana unto Sakara-Malik. The Lord also revealed that he had come to Ramkali only to meet and bless the two of them. He assured them of his protection and asked them not to worry. He added that they would be delivered very soon. The two brothers then sought the blessings of the Vaishnava's presence. Before leaving, however, they humbly cautioned the Lord that although the nabob was favorable at that moment, his mood was unpredictable. By nature, the nabob was a very dangerous and a very violent man. Hence, they suggested that it would be better if Mahaprabhu departed from that place without further delay. They also added that it would not be very appropriate for Mahaprabhu to travel to Vrindavan, accompanying such thousands of followers. Instead, it would be more befitting if Mahaprabhu went alone, accompanied by a select few. Having spoken thus, the two brothers offered prayers unto the lotus feet of the Lord and returned to their homes. Although Sri Chaitanya was the Supreme Lord himself, yet he acted like a mere human. In order to teach us neophytes how to react in such situations, heeding the advice of Rupa and Sanatana, he decided to leave that village.
Themes: renunciation, detachment, service
 
URL: https://youtu.be/sDOmqVy2GoY?t=791
 
Content-type: story
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: In the morning, he left for a place called Kanenaarsala, where he relished many past times of Lord Krishna. Then, thinking about the words of Sanatana, Mahaprabhu concluded that it would indeed not be proper to travel to Vrindavan with so many followers as it might disturb the atmosphere of the dhamma. Thus, he decided to return to Jagannath Puri. On returning to Puri, the Lord disclosed to his intimate associates how he had bestowed his most intimate mercy upon Rupa and Sanatana, owing to their humility and pure devotional qualities. Rupa Goswami took all his treasures and loaded them into a boat. He and his younger brother, Anupama, left for their ancestral home at Fateh Bar, presently in Bangladesh, where they distributed all their wealth. He appointed a brahmana to perform auspicious ceremonies so that they might obtain the shelter of Mahaprabhu. Of his wealth, he gave 50% charity to the brahmanas and Vaishnavas and 25% to his relatives. Srila Prabhupada highly glorifies and hails this as a perfect example of how one should divide his money and retire from one's household life. He also deposited 10,000 gold coins with a grocer in order to help Sanatana Goswami to escape. Sri Rupa Goswami kept one-fourth of his wealth aside with a respectable brahmana for his personal safety and emergencies. Srila Rupa Goswami then set out with two men to find out where Mahaprabhu was, when Mahaprabhu would be leaving for Vrindavan from Jagannath Puri. The men came back with news that Lord Chaitanya had already left. Sri Rupa immediately wrote a letter to Sanatana Goswami informing him of the news. He also added that he and Anupama were living immediately from Vrindavan and asked Sanatana Goswami to set off as well. He also informed his elder brother that he had left 10,000 gold coins with a grocer to help him escape.
Themes: liberation, instruction, empowerment
 
URL: https://youtu.be/sDOmqVy2GoY?t=931
 
Content-type: story
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: Meanwhile, Sanatana Goswami stopped going to the office and would just remain at home reading and chanting Srimad Bhagavatam all day. In the assembly of devotees, Nawab Hussain Shah came to his residence to enquire about Sanatana's health as he thought he was sick. Seeing that he was perfectly fit, the Nawab asked Sanatana to assist him in attacking the province of Orissa. Sanatana Goswami refused to obey the order of the king and expressed his desire to retire from service instead. The king became enraged and ordered Sanatana's arrest. Using the money that Rupa Goswami had kept for him, Sanatana Goswami managed to bribe the jailer and somehow escaped. The jailer was initially reluctant to release him, but 7,000 gold coins were enough to change his position. On being released, Sanatana immediately crossed over the Ganges and fled. He passed through hilly terrains and spent the night at a small hotel on his way to Benares. The hotel owner, knowing that Sanatana's servant Isha had eight gold coins in his possession, wanted to murder both of them and take away the money. However, Isha could guess the evil plans of the hotel owner and voluntarily offered him the coins and sought his help to safely reach his destination. Thus, the owner helped them cross the hilly track and proceed towards Varanasi. The two brothers, Rupa and Anupama, meanwhile, met with Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu at Prayag, Allahabad. The two of them had straws between their teeth and surrendered themselves at the lotus feet of the Lord. Lord Chaitanya embraced the two brothers and placed his feet upon the head. It was here at Prayag, at the Dvadasva Medha Ghat, that Lord Chaitanya had instructed Sri Rupa Goswami for ten days, empowering him in the philosophy of devotional service. He taught Rupa the limit of Krishna Tattva, Bhakti Tattva, the truth regarding the transcendental mellows, culminating in conjugal love between Radha and Krishna, Krishna Tattva, Bhakti Tattva, Rasa Tattva, Pranata.
Themes: literature, devotional service, essence
 
URL: https://youtu.be/sDOmqVy2GoY?t=1089
 
Content-type: philosophy
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: He also taught him the ultimate conclusions of Srimad Bhagavatam. By entering into Rupa's heart, Mahaprabhu empowered him to ascertain the conclusions of all truth. Teaching him thus, Lord Chaitanya sent Rupa Goswami to Vrindavan, instructing him to excavate the holy places of Krishna's pastimes, discover the ancient deities that lay hidden in the Vrindavan and reinstall them in his temples, write transcendental pieces of literature and preach the Vrindavan way of worship to the entire world. The summary of Lord Chaitanya's teaching to Rupa Goswami has been summarized by Srila Rupa in the teachings of Lord Chaitanya. So those who want to read more about the teachings can read the teachings of Lord Chaitanya or Chaitanya Chaita Amrita. It is said that Lord Chaitanya had taught the four principal qualities of a Vaishnava through four of his intimate associates. He had taught the quality of self-control through the life and examples of Ramananda Raya, the quality of neutrality through Damodara Pandita who even scolded Mahaprabhu at times, the aspect of tolerance through Haridas Thakur and the quality of humility through Sanatana Goswami. Among the books that Shri Sanatana Goswami had written, Hari Bhakti Vilas and Brihat Vaishnava Toshani are significant. Amongst the books composed by Shri Rupa Goswami, Hamsa Dutta, Uddhava Sandesha, Krishna Janma Tithi Vidhi, Radha Krishna Ganodesha Deepika, Sthava Mala, Vidakta Madhava, Lalita Madhava, Dhanakeli Kaumotuki, Bhakti Rasamata Sindhu, Ujjvala Neelamani, Akshyata Chandrika, Mathura Mahima, Padhyavali, Nataka Chandrika, and Laghu Bhagavata Amrita are the most significant. Bhakti Rasamata Sindhu contains the essence of all the revealed scriptures. Shri Srinivas Acharya in his prayers unto the six Goswamis hence glorifies them as expert in extracting the essence of all the revealed scriptures with the aim of establishing the Jeeva in his eternal position of performing pure devotional service unto Krishna.
Themes: pastimes, worship, legacy

Content: Both Rupa and Sanatana travelled extensively through Vraja Mandala, discovering many pastimes, places of Radha Madhava. Having no fixed residence, they would catch a few wings of sleep, no more than one and a half hours per day, under a tree or bush. They used to live by whatever little they obtained, by performing Madhukari, Shri begging from door to door. Shri Rupa and Sanatana spent all their time writing, discussing Krishna pastimes, and chanting Krishna's holy names. Shri Rupa Goswami established the worship of Shri Radha Govinda Dev in Vrindavan, while Shri Sanatana inaugurated the worship of Shri Madan Mohana. Both of these enchanting deities are currently being worshipped in Rajasthan. So the different things we see in Ramkeli here are the temples. That is this temple where we are sitting. That is Radha Madan Mohan Deity, established by Rupa and Sanatana. We also have Balarama and Revati. And they also established Chaitanya. Three Deities of the Pancha Tattva. Pancha Tattva have five personalities. But installed three personalities here. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu in the middle. Nityananda on the left. And Advaita on the right. So three personalities they installed here. There is this Keli Kadamba tree, under which Chaitanya Mahaprabhu met Rupa and Sanatana. And there is a small temple. And the entrance of Govinda Hari's lotus feet. There is Rupa Sagar, a large pond excavated, by Sri Rupa Goswami. We will see here. There are also eight sacred ponds, little far away around this place. Just outside the temple gate, is a beautiful deity of Lord Chaitanya, that had been installed over here, by Jeeva Goswami. The dancing hall surrounding this temple, has been renovated and restored to its health, with help of, here also, Bhaktivedanta Goswami's charitable trust, has given money to renovate this temple, Srila Prabhupada's trust. There is also the prison house, where Sanatana Goswami was present, imprisoned by the Nava. Then there is also, a little away, a Rose Minar, which was constructed by Sanatana Goswami. Themes: Humility, Material Fall, Surrender
 
URL: https://youtu.be/jsbOXtHRf6Y?t=68
 
Content-type: philosophy
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: Text 98. Putting a straw in his mouth and bowing down, Sanatana Goswami clasped the lotus feet of Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and humbly spoke as follows. Text 98. Putting a straw in his mouth and bowing down, Sanatana Goswami clasped the lotus feet of Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and humbly spoke as follows. Translation. Sanatana Goswami said, I was born in a low family and my associates are all low class men. I myself am fallen and am the lowest of men. Indeed, I have passed my whole life fallen in the well of sinful materialism. Actually, Shri Sanatana Goswami belonged to a brahmana family because he belonged to the Saraswati division of the brahmanas and was well-cultured and well-educated. Somehow or other, he accepted a ministership in the Muslim government. Therefore, he had to associate with meat-eaters, drunkards and cross-materialists. Sanatana Goswami considered himself fallen for in the association of such men, he also fell victim to material enjoyment. Having passed his life in that way, he considered that he had wasted his valuable time. This statement about how one can become fallen in this material world is made by the greatest authority in the Gaudiya Vaishnavasam Pradaya. Actually, the whole world is presently fallen into material existence. Everyone is a meat-eater, drunkard, woman-hunter, gambler and what not. People are enjoying material life by committing the four basic sins. Although they are fallen, if they simply submit themselves at the lotus feet of Shri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, they will be saved from sinful reactions. This ends the Bhaktivedanta Parbat.

He would chant 300,000 names of Lord Shri Krishna every day. He would cry. He would dance. Sometimes he would fall unconscious chanting the names of Lord Shri Krishna. Villagers would come here to take association of Haridasa Thakur and chant with him. But the villagers also noticed there was a black poisonous snake living in the same cave which posed a great threat to Haridasa and other visitors. So they were very hesitant to visit the place. Understanding their fear, Haridasa Thakur spoke to the snake and told them, My dear snake, because of the fear of you, devotees are hesitant to come here and chant with me. If you don't mind, could you please go somewhere else and live or if you are uncomfortable leaving this place, I would like to go and be somewhere else. With the words of Haridas Thakur, the black snake immediately left the place and went away. Seeing this miracle, the villagers were struck with awe and reverence. Their respect and devotion to Haridas Thakur increased immensely. This wonderful pastime happened at this place where Haridas Thakur stayed and performed his Nama bhajana.

The prescribed duties are already there. Therefore perfect human society means, first of all, there must be this division. Brāhmaṇa, kṣatriya, vaiśya. And when they act the duties of that particular position, brāhmaṇa, kṣatriya, for the satisfaction of Krsna, that is perfection of life. It doesn't matter whether you are a śūdra or you are brāhmaṇa, but if you act for the satisfaction of Krsna according to the prescription of your position, then your life is perfect. That is wanted.

One cannot reach the real point of factual knowledge without being helped by the right person, one who is already established in that knowledge. One who is always engaged in sinful activities must utilize the process of ablation, called the Prayaschitta. Without doing so, one surely will be transferred to the right path. One who is engaged in sinful activities will not be transferred to the left path. One who has been engaged in sinful activities should be transferred to hellish planets in order to undergo a miserable life as a result of sinful activities. This is Bhaktivedanta's purport. Themes: Absolute Truth, Bhagavan, Brahman, Paramatma
 
URL: https://youtu.be/e3SxWTcHSKk?t=48
 
Content-type: philosophy
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: Of life they do not lead to higher planets but to infamy report by Srila Prabhupada. Krishna and the Supreme Personality of Godhead are identical, therefore Lord Krishna is referred to as Bhagawan throughout the Gita. Bhagawan is the ultimate in the Absolute Truth. Absolute Truth is realized in three phases of understanding, namely Brahman or the impersonal all-pervasive spirit, Paramatma or the centralized aspect of the Supreme within the heart of all living entities, and Bhagawan or the Supreme Personality of Godhead. In the Srimad Bhagavatam this conception of the Absolute Truth is explained thus: vadanti tattva vidas tattvam yajnana madhvayam brahmeti paramatmeti bhagavaneti shabdhyate. The Absolute Truth is realized in three phases of understanding by the knower of the Absolute Truth and all of them are identical. Such phases of the Absolute Truth are expressed as Brahman, Paramatma, and Bhagawan. These three Divine aspects can be explained by the example of the sun, which also has three different aspects, namely the sunshine, the sun's surface, and the sun planet itself. One who studies the sunshine only is the preliminary student, one who understands the sun's surface is further advanced, and one who can enter into the sun planet is the highest. Ordinary students who are satisfied by simply understanding the sunshine, its universal pervasiveness, and the glaring effulgence of its impersonal nature may be compared to those who can realize only the Brahman feature of the Absolute Truth. The student who has advanced further can know the sun disc, which is compared to knowledge of the Paramatma feature of the Absolute Truth, and the student who can enter into the heart of the Sun planet is compared to those who realize the personal features of the Supreme Absolute Truth. Therefore the bhaktas or the transcendentalists who have realized the Bhagawan feature of the Absolute Truth are the top most transcendentalists. Although all students who are engaged in the study of the Absolute Truth are engaged in the same subject matter, the sunshine, the sun disc, and the inner affairs of the Sun planet cannot be separated from one another, and yet the students of the three different phases are not in the same category. The Sanskrit word Bhagawan is explained by the great authority Parashara Muni, the father of Vyasa Deva: the Supreme personality who possesses all riches, all strength, all fame, all beauty, all knowledge, and all renunciation is called Bhagawan.
Themes: Krishna's Supremacy, Aryan Values, Liberation

Content-type: philosophy

In the bhagavatham also there is a list of many incarnations of the Supreme personality of Godhead, but Krishna is described as the original personality of Godhead from whom many many incarnations and personalities of Godhead expand. Krishna is the one who is eternal and full of knowledge and bliss, he is the Supreme personality whom many incarnations and personalities of Godhead expand. All the lists of the incarnations of Godhead submitted herewith are either plenary expansions or parts of the plenary expansions of the Supreme Godhead, but Krishna is the Supreme Personality of Godhead Himself. Therefore, Krishna is the original Supreme Personality of Godhead, the Absolute Truth, the source of both the Supersoul and the Impersonal Brahman. In the presence of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Arjuna's lamentation for his kinsmen is certainly unbecoming, and therefore Krishna expressed His surprise with the word, kutas, wherefrom? Such unmanly sentiments were never expected from a person belonging to the civilized class of men known as Aryans. The word Aryan is applicable to persons who know the value of life and have a civilization based on spiritual realization. Persons who are led by the material conception of life do not know that the aim of life is really to achieve the goal of life. They are led by the realization of the Absolute Truth, Vishnu or Bhagavan, and they are captivated by the external features of the material world, and therefore they do not know what liberation is. Persons who have no knowledge of liberation from material bondage are called non-Aryans. Although Arjuna was a Kshatriya, he was deviating from his prescribed duties by declining to fight. This act of cowardice is described as befitting the non-Aryans. Such deviation from duty does not help one in the program, nor does it even give one the opportunity to become famous in this world. Lord Krishna did not approve of the so-called compassion of Arjuna for his kinsmen.

In other words, any person ordered by his guru to become guru is authorized. And any person who is not ordered by his guru, but he becomes guru, some or the other, either by himself or somebody else tells him, or there is a body of some people who give him a no objection to become a guru, some or the other, he becomes guru, then he is not bona fide, he is rubber-stamped or unauthorized guru.

And there in Rishikesh, they had taken a place on rent and some few devotees and Prabhupada was staying there. While they were staying there, on the 14th of May, in the night, there was a fierce thunderstorm. There was a power cut. It was pitch black in the night and there was lightning, thunder and strong winds were blowing the whole night. Devotees were all shaken up. The next day, on May 15th, Srila Prabhupada called the devotees and instructed them to make his will. So now, this was a clear indication that Srila Prabhupada had decided to leave the material world.

Themes: divine will, devotee
 
URL: https://youtu.be/weLxSeRIE6Q?t=34
 
Content-type: philosophy
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: Translation: The Lord then said to His attendants, Jaya and Vijaya, depart this place, but fear not. All glories unto you. Though I am capable of nullifying the Brahmanas' curse, I would not do so. On the contrary, it has my approval. Purport: As explained in connection with Text No. 26, all the incidents that took place had the approval of the Lord. Ordinarily, there is no possibility that the four sages could be so angry with the Dharmasur, nor could the Supreme Lord neglect His two doorkeepers, nor can one come back from the Vaikuntha after taking birth there. All these incidents, therefore, were designed by the Lord Himself for the sake of His pastime in the material world. Thus, He plainly says that it was done with His approval. Otherwise, it would have been impossible for inhabitants of Vaikuntha to come back to this material world simply because of Brahmanical curse. The Lord especially blesses the so-called culprits. All glories unto you. A devotee, once accepted by the Lord, can never fall down. That is the conclusion of this incident. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta Purport.

This purse is mine. So now he wanted to ascertain, it really belongs to him, or he is just simply for the sake of, because, what's the harm in getting a purse? Yeah, it belongs to me. So he says, what's the proof? What's the evidence? What's the identity that this belongs to you? So he says, in that purse, there is a picture of Lord Krishna. So he says, anyone, Krishna is so famous, anyone can have a picture of Krishna in the purse. By the way, why don't you have your picture? Generally in the purse, people have, you know, their picture. By the way, why don't you have your picture in the purse? So, on that, this person became little, you know, very, sad voice, he said, let me tell you the reason why I don't have my picture. He says, when I was young, when I was in school, my father gifted this purse to me. And, naturally, I was very, very, dear to my father. When I went to the college, he used to send lot of money to me, pocket money. And, because I was attached to my father, I put the picture of my father in the purse. And that remained for many years, till, I grew up. And, I became enamoured by my looks. I had nice thick black hairs, nice haircut. And then, one day, I replaced the picture of my father with, my picture. And then, years rolled by, I got married. I had a beautiful wife. She was very handsome. And, one day, I replaced, my picture with my, wife's picture. And, I used to spend lot of time, seeing that picture. She was very, very handsome. Very, very beautiful. After marriage, we got a beautiful child. Very cute. And, I got so attached to him. Many a times, just to spend time with him, I would go late to the office, and come back early, to play with that child. And, naturally, out of affection, the child picture was placed in the purse. And, now, many years back, my father passed away. After few years, even my wife, expired. My son now has grown up. He has got married. He has his family. He is staying separately. He hardly remembers me now. He hardly comes to meet me. Now, I am all alone. Tautu Girvana Rishabhau, Dustara Dharidokataha, Hatashriyau Brahmashapad, Abhutam Vigatasamayu. Translation: But those two gatekeepers, the best of the demigods, their beauty and luster diminished by the curse of the brahmanas, became morose and fell from Vaikuntha, the abode of the Supreme Lord. Translation: Then as Jaya and Vijaya fell from the Lord's abode, a great roar of disappointment arose from all the demigods who were sitting in the splendid airplanes. Text No. 35. Tau eva hi adhuna, taap tau, paashada paravarao hari, diter jathara nirvishthyam, diter jathara nirvishthyam, kashyapam teja ulbanam. Translation and purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swamishila Prabhupada. Translation: Lord Brahma continued, those two principal doorkeepers of the personality of Godhead have now entered the womb of Diti, the powerful semen of Kashyapa Muni, having covered them. Purport: Here is a clear proof of how a living entity coming originally from Vaikuntha Loka is encaged in material elements. The living entity takes shelter within the semen of a father, which is injected within the womb of a mother, and with the help of the mother's emulsified ovium, the living entity grows a particular type of body. In this connection it is to be remembered that the mind of Kashyapa Muni was not in order when he conceived the two sons, Hiranyakasha and Hiranyakashyapu. Therefore the semen he discharged was simultaneously extremely powerful and mixed with the quality of anger. It is to be concluded that while conceiving a child, the mind of Kashyapa Muni was not in order. One's mind must be very sober and devotional. For this purpose the Garbhodana Samskara is recommended in the Vedic scriptures. If the mind of the father is not sober, the semen discharge will not be very good. Thus the living entity wrapped in the matter produced from the father and mother will be demoniac like Hiranyakasha and Hiranyakashyapu. The conditions of conception are to be carefully studied. This is a very great thing. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta. So here in this verse it is being mentioned how Jaya and Vijaya, their lustre diminished by the curse of the Brahmanas, became morose and fell from Vaikuntha, the board of the Supreme Lord. So in this particular case, Jaya and Vijaya, they grossly, literally fell from the spiritual world, they came to the material world. Fall down can be very gross, in the literal sense, somebody leaving the association of devotees. We have examples of some devotees who were there, but for various reasons, they left the association of devotees. So this is an example of a gross fall down. Somebody may leave the association of devotees, may cease to be a devotee, goes back to his old ways of life, old ways of eating, old association, prior to his coming to Krishna consciousness. And there could be also subtle fall downs. Subtle fall downs could be the fall down in the consciousness. There is a spiritual platform, and there is a mental platform, and there is a material platform. On a daily basis, we do our sadhana, chanting, reading, hearing, the various aspects in the morning sadhana, to give us the spiritual strength, so that we can be situated in the spiritual platform. So gross fall down can be very gross, where someone becomes so infected, that he can leave the association of devotees. And on a daily basis, yes, there will be ups and downs as far as consciousness is concerned. Sometimes somebody is not careful, whole consciousness dips, and he is totally at the material platform, completely bewildered, with complete material consciousness. Material calculations, fear, anxiety, what will happen to me, all material calculations can overpower a person. So Prabhupada in one place says, anyone of us can be arrested by Maya at any moment. This is a baseline. Anyone of us can be arrested by Maya at any moment. Any moment. If we are not strongly attached to Krishna consciousness.

Still Maya is attacking me. Still I am falling down. It will happen. Only check we should be, am I taking the medicine sincerely? Am I seriously following the process? Or am I doing some short circuits? Am I, just like today we read, am I partially following the process? Just like you go to a doctor, the doctor says, take this course for ten days, you take for one day, and you say, leave it. Enough. So if we are following the process, even though we may be overpowered by Maya, we come in illusion, we fall down occasionally, we should get up, have courage, have conviction that, Prabhupada is with us. If we follow his instructions, he will protect me. Avashya Rakshivya Krishna. E Vishwasa. We don't have to live in fear and anxiety. Nor we should go to the other extreme of being complacent. So this right balance, if we do that, we can practice this spiritual life and come out of this illusion. Let's stop here.
Themes: Hiranyakashipu, benediction, pride
 
URL: https://youtu.be/yp6RiH3xOYA?t=0
 
Content-type: philosophy
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto No. 3, Chapter No. 17, Text No. 19. Translation and purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swamishlaprabhupada. Translation: The elder child, Rinukashipu, was unafraid of death from anyone within the three worlds because he received a benediction from Lakshman. He was proud and puffed up due to his benediction and was able to bring all three planetary systems under his control. Purport: As will be revealed in later chapters, Rinukashipu underwent severe austerity and penance to satisfy Brahma and thus received a benediction of immortality. Actually, it is impossible even for Lord Brahma to give anyone the benediction of being immortal. But indirectly, Rinukashipu received the benediction that no one within this material world would be able to kill him. In other words, because he originally came from the abode of Vaikuntha, he was not to be killed by anyone within this material world. The Lord desired to appear himself to kill him. One may be very proud of his material advancement in knowledge, but he cannot be immune to the four principles of material existence, namely, birth, death, old age, and disease. It was Lord's plan to teach people that even Rinukashipu, who was so powerful and strongly built, could not live more than his destined duration of life. One may become as strong and puffed up as Rinukashipu and bring under his control all the three worlds, but there is no possibility of continuing life eternally or keeping the conquered booty forever. So many emperors, who have ascended to Paa, and they are now lost in oblivion, that is the history of the world. Thus ends the Bhagavad Gita. So here in this verse, Rinukashipu, who had received a benediction from Lord Brahma, and the benediction was that no one within this material world would be able to kill him. So Prabhupada mentions that although Brahma cannot give the benediction of Trinakashrupa being immortal, but indirectly he got that benediction. And because of the benediction that he cannot be killed by anyone in the material world, he was proud and puffed up, and was able to bring all three planetary systems under his control. He was also unafraid of death from anyone within the three worlds.
Themes: humility, pride, instrument

Unafraid of death. One time there was a lady, she had in her home, her grandchild who had come from a distant place for a vacation. So every morning when the child would get up, he would come to his grandmom and she would be writing something. Maybe she was an author or something. She would be always writing something, always with a paper, and she would write with a pencil. So one day this child, this small child became inquisitive and asked, what are you writing? Is it about me? Is it about us? So the lady said, what I am writing is relatively unimportant. But since you have asked the question, let me tell you that this pencil with which I am writing, whatever I am writing, it is playing a very, very important role. And before you leave, after the vacation gets over, I will gift you this pencil. So the child, you know, he had been also writing with pencil, so he was a little surprised. What's the big deal about a pencil? How is it different from the rest of pencils which is available in the market so easily? He said, I will give you this pencil and I want you to imbibe, you will imbibe the five qualities what you see in this pencil for the rest of your life. And if you do that, you will become a great man. So again the child was somehow intrigued what the mother is, what the grandmother is trying to tell. So she says, the first quality in the pencil, you see it is an ordinary pencil, but the pencil just remembers that there is a hand guiding it. Using a pencil, a writer, an author might write some wonderful text like Shakespeare wrote, wonderful, you know, whatever his writings, his poems, so famous he is for all his writings, he would have used a pen or a pencil to write it. But the credit goes to the Shakespeare. Although, what was literally written in the paper was written by a pen, but the pen or a pencil never took any credit. It was always conscious, had it been conscious, assuming that the pen is conscious or a pencil is conscious, it is very, very clear that I am being used. Something was being used, something wonderful may come up, but it will never ever even think of taking a credit. So likewise, a pencil always remembers that there is a hand guiding it. Here, Himata Shubha, we see, is getting puffed up, is proud, because he is not able to see the hand. Even when we become proud, or when we achieve something, or when we accomplish something, or when we do something big in the service of the Lord, unless we remember that we are just nimitta mātra, we are just an instrument in the hand of the Lord, actually all credit goes to the Lord, unless we consciously remember, unless we consciously give due credit to the Lord. sarvasya cāhaṁ vidhi-saṇi viṣṭo matasme ter jñānaṁ apok. The ideas, the thought, the inspiration came from the Lord. Unless we remember that, we will also become proud. So here we are saying, Himata Shubha is proud because he is totally oblivious of that he is amsa of the Lord. The Lord is the supreme controller. Because he has got three planetary systems under his control, he is thinking he is great, he is immortal. Nobody can touch him.

Why the devotee is not attached to the results, it is not some artificial imposition in the mind, because of this consciousness that it is being done by the Lord through him. He is just an instrument, just like the pencil, just an instrument. The second quality of the pencil is, it learns to bear, it learns to bear pains and sorrows. After every few hours of writing, the person who is holding the pencil, the grandmother would use a sharpener and sharpen the pencil. And although the pencil may go through some pain, when you are, so to say, being sharpened, but after that pain, after that so-called suffering, the pencils become sharp, and it drives better. So likewise, a devotee also, with his Kṛṣṇa consciousness, is willing to go through pains and pleasures, sorrows, knowing that the Lord is his well-wisher, knowing that Avashya Rakshabhi Kṛṣṇa, knowing that the Lord is in control of my life, knowing that the Lord is taking me through all this to make me a better devotee, to make me more dependent on Him, same as the case being for Kunti Mahārāj, the Lord gives us more and more troubles. Unlike Rina Kashyapu here, who is trying to ward off all troubles of birth, death, old age, disease, by becoming immortal, a devotee of the Lord does not make such endeavours. He is rather endeavouring to be dependent on the Lord. Knowing the dangers in the material world, this material world which Kṛṣṇa says is duḥ khālaya māśāśvatam, rather than investing all his time and energy making this place a wonderful place to stay in comfortably eternally, realising that it is temporary and it is a place of misery, he invests his time and energy in becoming conscious of the Lord, dependent on the Lord. Even if he goes through some pains and pleasures, he takes it in stride, learns the lessons, depends on Kṛṣṇa more and more, and becomes a better devotee. So the pencil also, when it is sharpened, it does not rebel. It allows to be sharpened, although it goes through some pain, although it gets shortened, but in the process, it becomes better, better in writing.

The whole process of bhakti is somehow to give up this pride, to give up this enjoying tendency and come back to our constitutional position of being a servant of the Lord.

What will be the symptoms of people in Kali Yuga? So Krishna instead of replying, he took four arrows and shot those arrows in four different directions and told them, kindly go and get me those arrows. You will get answers to your question. So Arjuna went in one direction in search of arrow and finally he saw the arrow and he saw a very cute cuckoo bird singing very melodiously. Cuckoo birds, the sound is very very pleasant. So Arjuna was, when he heard that voice, he was charmed, so to say, by that sweet little word from the cuckoo bird. When he went near the bird, near the tree, he saw the cuckoo bird actually was eating the flesh of a live rabbit and the rabbit was wriggling in pain. Somehow he was surprised what kind of scene he is seeing. Cuckoo bird, sweet little bird, supposed to sing very melodiously and here is eating flesh of a live rabbit and the rabbit is wriggling in pain. He immediately took the arrow and came back to Krishna, not able to comprehend what he has seen. Then Bhima went in another direction. He saw the five wells. Four wells and the one well in between these four wells. Each of these four wells is swimming with water. Water is overflowing, sweet little water is overflowing from the wells and the well in the center, the fifth well is completely dry, completely empty. He was also surprised by this sight. What is this? All five wells are together, four wells are overflowing with water and the fifth well is completely dry. Not able to comprehend, he comes back. Nakula goes in another direction, tries to pick up the arrow and here he sees a cow has given birth to a baby calf or whatever calf and after birth it seems the mother cow licks the calf till it gets clean and keeps on licking the calf, keeps on licking. Even though the calf has become completely clean, keeps on licking. Finally, the cowman has to pull the calf away from the cow. By then, the calf had become wounded. There were wounds in the body because of excessive licking by the mother cow. So he was also surprised, how can mother cow hurt her own calf? And Sahadev goes in another direction. He picks up the arrow, it's at the foothills of a hill, big mountain and he sees from the top of the mountain a big boulder, big rock is rolling down the hill. Big boulder. And as it's rolling down, all the big, big trees, rocks, whichever is coming on the way, it's smashing it. It's smashing all things which comes in its way. And finally the boulder is coming down. And to his surprise, when it almost reaches the down, there is small little grass and the boulder stops. Stops rolling down and stops. That small little grass holds the boulder. Again he is bewildered, what's going on? Big, big trees are uprooted and small little grass has stopped this boulder and it's not moving down.

URL: https://youtu.be/gVyhsE-q17U?t=0
 
Content-type: philosophy
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: OM NAMO BHAGWATE VASUDEVAYA. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto No. 3, Chapter No. 18, Text No. 6: SATURBYAMANU ARIDURUKKATOMARAYA, TAMSTRAGRAH GAMGAM APALAKSHABHITAM, TODAMRISHAN NIRGAD AMBUMADYAD, RAHATASAKARENUR YATHEBHA, RAHATASAKARENUR YATHEBHA. Translation and purport by His Divine Eminence, A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada. Translation: Although the Lord was pained by the sharp-like abusive words of the demons, He bore the pain. But seeing that the earth on the ends of His tusk was frightened, He rose out of the water, just as an elephant emerges with its female companion when assailed by an alligator. Purport: The Mayavadi philosophers cannot understand that the Lord has feelings. The Lord is satisfied if someone offers Him a nice prayer. And similarly, if someone decries His existence or calls Him by ill names, God is dissatisfied. The Supreme Personality of Godhead is decried by the Mayavadi philosophers who are almost demons. They say that God has no head, no form, no existence, and no legs, hands, or other bodily limbs. In other words, they say that He is dead or lame. All this is not true. All these misconceptions of the Supreme Lord are a source of dissatisfaction to Him. He is never pleased with such atheistic descriptions. In this case, although the Lord felt sorrow from the piercing words of the demons, He delivered the earth for the satisfaction of the demigods, who are ever His devotees. The conclusion is that God is as sentient as we are. He is satisfied by our prayers and dissatisfied by our harsh words against Him. In order to give protection to His devotees, He is always ready to tolerate insulting words from the Atheists. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta purport.

He is a sentient being. Just like we have feelings, we are sentient beings, we are amsa of the Lord. We being amsa of the Lord, the Lord Himself, who is Purna, who is the source of all these amsas, or who is the complete whole, the absolute truth, He is also a person, a sentient being. And Prabhupada mentions, because he has feelings, bhavag rahayu janardana. When someone offers prayers, the Lord is very pleased. We being amsa, we also have the tendency, if someone calls us by our name, or someone praises us, or glorifies us, we also feel some pleasure. Although that's not our constitutional position, to be glorified. However, we are the predominators, and our constitutional position is to glorify the predominator. So just like, to give a crude example, we also experience pain and pleasure. If someone praises us, we feel pleasure. If someone criticizes us, passes a sarcastic remark, we feel pain. So here, the demon used abusive words and it pained the Lord. All said and done, the demon also is amsha of the Lord. And the Lord is said to be Shad Aishwarya Purnam. He has unlimited strength, unlimited wealth, unlimited knowledge, unlimited fame, unlimited beauty, unlimited renunciation. And other good qualities, which we see, is also there.
