We went to the Chief Minister's office. Met some personal secretaries, the officers in charge of various protocols. And by and large, we were quite unsuccessful. Even though we had some contacts, it was quite difficult. And every time we would go to the Chief Minister's office, we would see a lot of people waiting for him. Lot of people wanting to meet him. And he was quite busy. A time came when I started personally feeling it is next to impossible. It's very, very difficult. Now mind you, I am talking about the Chief Minister of one of the 29 states in India. Now you can imagine, without the right connection, if we were to meet the Prime Minister of the country, Narendra Modi. Or for that matter, you extrapolate, if you were to meet the President of United States of America. We are talking about Krishna, who is not only the President or Chairman of a particular continent, a particular country, a particular universe, of all the universes. The Supreme Creator, the cause of all causes. So I was getting quite frustrated, quite disgusted. And a time came when I thought, okay, let us give it up. Not required. Hoga toh hoga, nahi hua toh nahi hua. Now Lord is watching. And he understood that we are quite eager to meet the Chief Minister. Although whatever we want from Chief Minister can easily be done by the Lord. But then there are some protocols we have to follow while we are in this world. The Lord has invested His power in different personalities and we have to respect them. That way Aveda says, even a king, who is quite powerful, is having the power on behalf of the Lord. He is ruling on behalf of the Lord. The Lord has empowered him. The Lord has empowered the different Devatas. They are not independent controllers. So, then we met one person. It seems he was very close to the Chief Minister. So, we told him that we want to meet him. And he said, when do you want to meet him? It was like, you know, asking as if we had a choice. So, I told him, sooner the better. So, then he asked me, are you free today evening? Imagine for days together, for months together, we were trying to meet him. And then he is asking, do you want to meet him today? I said, why not? We are ready. And then, he told us, okay, come at 4.30 in the evening. We went there. Again, like our previous experience, it was quite a busy scenario. Lot of people waiting. This person comes with us, walks directly to Chief Minister, and after two minutes, comes out and tells us, please come in. Please come with me. And we go with him, and for 15 minutes, we were with the Chief Minister, talking to him. Now, the point what I am trying to make is, the right connection is very important. For that matter, in everything in our lives, we all have experience of how the right connections make a big difference. That's how, in power circles, people always trying to connect with so-called power brokers, the people who wield power, people who have the right connections for material gains.

So, what time can vary from place to place depending on what time the sun is rising in that particular place. So, that 48 minutes period is called the Brahmamurta. 48 minutes, it lasts, it ends 48 minutes before sunrise and starts 96 minutes before sunrise. The total duration is 48 minutes.

Sometimes, it may appear that, you know, this is instilling fear in us. The Lord is saying, I am the death. I am fear personified. Surrender to me, otherwise you will be killed. Let's understand the context. So, everyone has to surrender. Now, what a devotee does is, he willingly surrenders. He accepts the supremacy of the Lord. Accepts that, yes, we are subordinates. Say to the Lord, yes, you are the master. Yes, you are the controller. Yes, we are controlled. Yes, we are not all powerful. We have some powers. We have some abilities. We can control few things. But you are the all powerful. You are the supreme controller. So, that way, with this knowledge and natural consequences, the devotee becomes humble and his dependency on the Lord increases. He depends on the Lord with the understanding that, if the Lord protects, I am safe. Not because I have lot of money. Not because I have lot of strength. If I have the mercy and grace of the Lord, I am safe. If I don't have the mercy of the Lord, in spite of having everything, I am not safe. What happens is, this is the meaning of illusion or Maya. When we have money, bank balance, near and dear ones, well-wishers around us, there is a sense of, I am safe, security. The true security is in understanding that Lord is with me. That's why a devotee actually is fearless.

Content: Just like it looks good for a person to be free and not follow any of these traffic laws. Why do I have to stop when the red light comes? Why do I have to pay taxes? Why do I have to pay water bill? It's good when I am free. Nobody, there is no laws binding me. But if I am not law abiding, if suppose I don't stop at the red light, the traffic police will harass me, will penalize me till I become, till I become, till I become law abiding. So, Lord has given us a free will, a choice. Yatha ichchasi tatha karu. Now you do what you want. He does not force us to obey Him. He does not force us to worship Him. He does not force us to desire the way He wants us to desire. He does not force. He has come and spoken the Gita. He has sent His representatives. They are telling us to act in this way, to surrender to Him, to remember Him. The choice is ours. So, so long as we violate the laws knowingly or unknowingly, just like you violate a law, the police will penalize you. In material laws, you can still go scot free if you are too smart. But in this laws, we cannot go scot free. On one side we want to be happy, unknowingly, unintentionally or sometimes intentionally, we are breaking the laws for our selfish enjoyment. I don't care. I like eating it, I will eat. If you don't like it, don't eat it. And then, the nature penalizes. Prakriti kriyamane gunai karmani sarvasya. This Prakriti is acting under the direction of Maya adhyakshena prakriti suyatesa achara acharam. Under my direction, Prakriti is acting. It is not acting independently. Tsunami is not happening on its own. So, we violate the laws. Just like government is not hell bent on punishing us. Government has not created prison house ke mujhe maja aa raha hai. But there are some people who will violate the laws. So, the prison house is there. There is a department to punish such people, so that they mend their ways and become law abiding citizens. So, bahu naam janma naam ante. After many many lives of trial and error, trying to be happy, independent of the law, trying to secure ourselves, trying to do everything independent of the law, Gyanvaan, a person who is sufficiently experienced, who has sufficiently realized, maam prapadyante, will surrender to me willingly. And what will be his understanding? Vasudevam sarvam iti sa mahatma. That Atma is Mahanatma and Durlabha. A very rare person. A very rare person will come to that understanding. And how? After many many lifetimes. So, why this world is created? This Dukha is there. We violate, we get a beating, which is also there in the outside. You violate a traffic law, you get a beating. You violate a state law, you get a beating. May not be beating physically, may be in some other time, penalty. You violate the income tax law, they will penalize you. So, this world is created for our purification. So, this world is created for our purification. We come to our senses. Cosmic manifestation is a chance for the conditioned soul to go back to home, back to Godhead. It's a chance.

The Lord is involved in terms of witness and sanction. If suppose somebody is hell bent on murdering someone, the Lord will sanction and who will be responsible? The soul. As you sow, so shall you reap. You have to face the consequences. Then we say why lord I am suffering. So the lord's witness is there and he is a sanctioner. When we become devotees of the Lord, another important element gets added. The Lord is witnesser, sanctioner, and director. The Lord does not hold the reins of our life in that sense. He does not direct us. He has given the laws which are impersonally acting. We have a free will. We make the choices. We act in a particular way and the karmic reactions follow. As you sow so shall you reap. In case of a devotee, this Lord does not act impersonally. The Lord intervenes in the life of a devotee. The Lord is witnesser, sanctioner and a director. As per our karmic reaction we should be very very opulent, very very prosperous but if the Lord senses that this will take him away from me, he is wanting on one side, he is chanting on one side, he is pleading that lord i want you, i want to surrender to you but simultaneously he is desiring and acting in a way so that he becomes very prosperous and he can get distracted and can go away from me. As per karmic reaction I should become very prosperous, he may not sanction and as per karmic reaction I have to suffer very badly, but the Lord decides no, at this juncture he will not be able to handle it, his faith in me can shake, he may not sanction. The law of karma acts impersonally, impersonally when we do not surrender to the Lord. When we start surrendering to the Lord, to that extent the Lord intervenes in our life and takes charge of our life. That's why it is called partha sarti physically just like the Lord took the reins of the chariots of arjuna the god of creationes the choice is ours do we want to give the reins of our life to the Lord or we want to run the life ourselves.

Content: Many years back, when I was studying, I remember having travelled from Kurukshetra to Delhi. I was doing my B.Tech from Kurukshetra REC. And I happened to travel by train and it was an unreserved bhogi. We could not get the reservation. In fact, it was a short distance. And I got into the unreserved bhogi. And typically, we all have some idea how the systems work in India. I was fortunate enough to get a seat in the unreserved bhogi. And I somehow sat very tightly so that I should not lose the seat. And in no time, the entire bhogi was jam-packed. There was not even space for people to move. Practically, the whole bhogi was like people on top of each other. And I remember halfway through, I had some bit of urge to go to the restroom, to the toilet. But somehow I resisted. A time came when it was inevitable. I had to go. But I am sitting in an unreserved bhogi. And the moment I get up, not sure if I will get the seat back. So I requested the person sitting next to me that for two minutes, I very specifically mentioned, for two minutes I am going, kindly help me with this seat. And I made sure that I have kept one handkerchief. That's a typical Indian system of booking a seat. So I kept the handkerchief, requested him kindly, please keep a watch on the seat. I'll just come back in two minutes. Somehow with great difficulty, I rushed to the toilet and I came back in two minutes. And to my surprise, there was a person sitting on that seat. A very hefty, well-built person. And the moment I requested that person, very politely, very gently I requested, Sir, I was sitting here, my seat. The moment he heard me, he looked at me with a stern face and in Hindi, he mentioned to me, not mentioned, I would say he remarked, Tere baap ki seat hai. And I was quite surprised. I said, just two minutes back I was sitting here, I am coming from Kurukshetra, all along I was sitting in this seat, I requested this gentleman also, I pointed to that person, don't you remember I had told you for two minutes, I am going, I am coming back. So this person remarked, this hefty person who was sitting on my seat, quote unquote my seat, said that this is an unreserved bogie. There is no reservation here. Do you have a reservation? Show me your reservation. So for a moment I was getting very upset, very angry, very irritated at that person. I was about to shout at him, get angry at him, but seeing his size, seeing my size, I somehow decided not to get angry, not to get into a brawl with him, a fight with him. Then, I started thinking, on one side, yes, I can keep quiet, maybe because I am weak, maybe because I am much smaller in size, maybe I cannot fight it out. And then my mind saying, but this is injustice. Actually, you were all along sitting in this seat, just went for two minutes, and how can he now claim that he will not get up?

At one point, we boarded this train called life, so called birthday, the day we took birth. And a day will come when we will deboard this train. URL: https://youtu.be/37c7ip5pmuw?t=877
 
Content-type: philosophy
 
Audience: Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
 
Content: There is another kind of attachment. That attachment is, attachment to results. Results of our actions. Many many places in the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna talks about, you have the right to do your duty, but you are not attached, do not be attached to the results of your activities. When we get overly attached to the results, when we want things to transpire in a particular way only, we are setting the game for misery or problems or difficulties in our life. Just like in chess, the game is set, the board is set. Likewise, if we are overly attached to outcomes, overly attached, things have to happen the way I want it to happen. Then only, I will be happy. We are setting the game of life, which will inevitably lead to difficulties or misery in our life. As I mentioned in a previous session, for the results of our activities, it is not just our endeavor. There are so many parameters which are involved. Krishna in the Gita says, ultimately it is Daivam, the super soul. So, this understanding that results of my actions, is not solely dependent on my endeavors alone, is again a very very important aspect which we have to keep in mind, so that we are not overly attached to the outcome of our actions.

URL: https://youtu.be/37c7ip5pmuw?t=985

Content: One time, there was a person who was carrying in his hand puffed rice. All of us know about puffed rice. He was carrying in his hand and he was passing through a forest. And, with both hands clasped, having the puffed rice, he was trying to hold on that puffed rice, ensuring that nothing slips out from his hand. And on that particular day, it was quite windy. Suddenly the wind started blowing and became very very so called something like a hurricane. So, this person clasped his hand even more tighter and tried holding on to that puffed rice, kept on holding, holding till little by little, because of that wind pressure, the rice started slipping out one by one, one by one from his hand till practically, practically nothing was left. And, while he was trying to hold very tightly, ensuring, realizing that he will not be able to hold, everything is slipping off, he held his hand up high in the air and said, Govindabhog, Govindabhog, Govindabhog, indicating to Govinda that I am offering this puffed rice to you. The lesson we can learn from this story is, everything will slip out from our hands. Either we will leave the money we have or the money will leave us. So, we are not going to waste our time on this. Therefore, in the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna many, many places talks about do not be attached to the results of your activities.

You know in a river, there are waves and the straws are floating. They are practically like blown with the waves. So for a while, the straws come together. And the next wave comes, they all get segregated. So we all, as souls, are individuals. In this life, in this particular life, we have come together as friends, brothers, relatives, father, mother. And then the wave of Kala will come, which is inevitable. We all will be again separated. Sometimes people say that, you know, why do you talk about things which are painful to hear, which are not very pleasant. Why do we talk about, yes, a time will come, relationships will break. Why don't we talk about some pleasant things of life? Yes, we can talk about pleasant things. There are many pleasant things in the Bhagavad Gita also Krishna mentions about. But let's not turn a blind eye to the realities of life. This is a reality. We are not talking about philosophy. We are talking about realities of life. A day will come when relationships will break. If we are overly attached to someone, more will be the pain, more will be the agony, more will be the distress, which we will have to undergo.

Haru grew up and one day while he was tilling the fields, he was in the farm, suddenly a person rushed to him and said, Haru is not feeling well, something has happened to him. And he rushed home and then he was not in a good condition and then some days passed by, his condition worsened, he was diagnosed with cholera and eventually he died. So everyone in the family were completely devastated, the wife could not be consoled, she was crying pathetically. And this person, this farmer, was very calm and composed. In fact next day he again went to his fields for working. So when he came back home, he saw his wife was even weeping very bitterly. So family members had assembled, the relatives had come home and one of them asked, why are you so calm? Why are you not crying? So he told, last night I remember I had a very very vivid dream. In the dream, I was a king, I was living in a palace and I had eight children. I vividly remember the details of the dream. I was in a palace, I was a king and I had eight children. And now, today, after knowing that Haru has passed away, I am actually bewildered. I am actually in a dilemma. I am actually confused. Should I moan for those eight children which I had in the dream when I was a king or should I moan for Haru who died yesterday?
Themes: detachment, life as a dream, soul
 
URL: https://youtu.be/37c7ip5pmuw?t=1963
 
Content-type: philosophy
 
Audience: Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
 
Content: The understanding we get from this story is that this life in one sense is like a prolonged dream. In the night, we have experience of dreams. We get dreams. Although the dream may be unreal, but the experience of the dream is real. That's why in the dream, if we have a dream where we are being chased by a tiger, at times we perspire. The experience is real, although the content may not be true. We go into a dream world, but the experience is real. Likewise, from the perspective of eternal soul, this life is like a dream. Experience is real, but from the perspective of the soul, this is my brother, this is my sister, this is my father, is like a dream. In that sense, it's like a prolonged dream. In the night, we have a dream for 2 hours, 3 hours, 4 hours, and we wake up. And even if we have a pleasant dream or unpleasant dream, we somehow reconcile. It was after all a dream. This life is nothing but an extended dream from the perspective of the soul. So that way, if we have this understanding that this life just like a dream, we don't get overly attached. Suppose in a dream we had, we got lot of riches, lot of money, we were living in a palatial house, big bungalow, we had many many cars, and suddenly we wake up, we don't start crying. We just reconcile with the fact that after all it was a dream. We don't get overly attached to the contents of the dream knowing that it's a dream.

It is the mind, it is the senses which is dictating us, and the bewildered soul is completely lost, not knowing where he is heading. So, as scriptures say, that mind, if it is controlled, is our best friend, but the same mind, if it is uncontrolled, is our worst enemy. What is spirituality? Spirituality is all about how we control our mind. How we control our senses. How we control our urges so that we can focus our mind on the Supreme, on the Lord.

I repeat that which you resist persists. So, now we have our senses, we have our mind and we have plethora of sense objects all around us, alluring us, tempting us. Now we try to restrain. At times suppress our feelings, suppress our emotions, suppress our urges and at times it can backfire. Yes we have to control. Scriptures are saying, Tapo divyam shudhye brahma saukhyam to anantam, we have to control. But many times we can control the wrong way. That which we try to resist, persist.

Semuanya ada di tangan kita. Kita bisa melakukan apa saja dan semuanya. Hanya itu, kita harus bekerja keras, bekerja dengan jujur, dan kita bisa mencapainya. Jadi, itu adalah satu pemahaman ekstrim. Pemahaman ekstrim lain adalah, semuanya disesuaikan. Jika ada di kisah saya, saya akan mendapatkannya. Jika tidak, saya tidak akan mendapatkannya. Itu ekstrim lainnya. Jika Anda ingat, saya telah berbagi dengan Anda, minggu lalu, mengenai contoh cowok yang ditutup ke pol dengan garis. Mari kita ambil contoh yang sama. Tetapi, ketika cowok itu berusaha bergerak lebih jauh dari 10 meter, garis itu menutupnya kembali atau memastikan gerakannya. Sama seperti itu, kita juga memiliki sesuatu yang dikirimkan oleh destinasi. Mereka adalah kekuatan yang berlaku pada kita. Dan, ada beberapa elemen kebebasan atau pilihan yang bisa kita lakukan. Jadi, ini adalah perkahwinan antara dua kekuatan ini. Kita memiliki pilihan di dalam diri kita. Sama seperti cowok, bisa bergerak secara bebas di radius 10 meter. Tetapi, lebih jauh dari 10 meter, garis itu akan mematikan cowok. Jadi, ada beberapa kekuatan yang mematikan cowok. Kami memiliki pilihan yang dikirimkan oleh destinasi. Tetapi, kita juga memiliki kekuatan bebas. Banyak orang bertanya, mengapa Tuhan memberi kita kekuatan bebas? Mengapa Tuhan memberi kita keputusan? Jika Tuhan ingin menguasai kehidupan kita, Tuhan adalah semuanya baik. Semuanya akan baik. Kita tidak akan memiliki orang-orang yang melakukan hal yang salah, membuat keputusan yang salah. Mengapa Tuhan telah memberi kita kekuatan bebas?

The same mind if it is uncontrolled is a worst enemy.

Sometimes people even ask as devotees or somebody who is practicing spirituality, why does he at times become angry or become greedy? The idea is that a person may be taking the medicine, but that does not mean that a person who has started taking the medicine should get cured that very day. It takes time. It takes time for a person to get diseased, and even if a person is taking the right medicine, it takes time for the disease to subside. Therefore in the Bhagavad Gita, Krishna also says, One who is practicing the process of Krishna Consciousness, even if at times he engages in some abominable acts, still he has to be considered saintly because he is rightly situated. He is taking the medicine. He is following the process. It may take some time. So here this person also was attending satsang. You may say if he was attending satsang, why is he getting perturbed? Yes, he was disturbed. Many times we cannot help it. Theoretically we may know it is wrong, but there is conditioning, there is habitual response. So this person was quite perturbed, was quite disturbed. He could not focus and the speaker could really make it out because he was in the front row and he was always little jittery and not comfortable. So one day he asked him after the session, Why are you so disturbed, look so agitated? So he clearly told Swamiji, What to tell? Unfortunately I lost my umbrella and I am so disturbed. I wonder why a person has stolen in such an auspicious gathering and somehow I am missing my umbrella. More so it is rainy days. I cannot afford to buy another one. So the Swamiji requested another satsangi, the person there in the satsang, Why don't you buy an umbrella for him? And next day the person got him a new umbrella. So for a moment the person was quite happy, he has got an umbrella much better than what he had earlier, much bigger, much better quality. And that day the person attended satsang. But next few days the speaker noticed that still the person is not happy, still the person is morose. Even though he has been gifted a new umbrella, what could be the cause of his moroseness? Again after the session one day this person, the speaker inquired from him, Why are you morose? I have somehow got a new umbrella for you, now you should be happy. So he said, Yes Swamiji, what to do? Always this thought bothers me. Had I not lost that umbrella, I would today be having two umbrellas. I somehow missed that umbrella, now I have only one. So this is a symptom where a person in spite of having enough, is not contented, is always greedy, is wanting more, is never satisfied. Therefore it is said, Earth provides enough to satisfy a man's need, but not for every man's greed.

The other said, wait, wait, don't eat, don't eat, it's poison. Don't you know it's poison? It's poisonous. Don't eat, we'll die. But the other head wanted to teach that head a lesson, kept on eating, ate that entire poisonous fruit and the bird, both the heads including, died. They found that the nurses in that ICU they were addicted and they were gambling and on each patient in the ICU they used to have betting done. Just like you have betting in sports. You bet which team will win by which, how many and there is always a ratio and basis that there is a risk involved and the gains proportionate gains depending on the risk involved. So you bet on a particular team. Bet on a particular horse. URL: https://youtu.be/GccuoDdnBGw?t=454
 
Content-type: analogy
 
Audience: Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
 
Content: To give you a crude example, a toddler, a child who is barely 6 months, 8 months old, the lap of mother is completely dependent on the mother. If by chance mother forgets, although the mother does not forget, by chance the mother forgets to feed the child, the child will simply cry. It has no means to find the food by itself and go and take it. And because the mother knows about this, she is always conscious of the child. She is always concerned, she is always anxious, now my baby must be feeling hungry, I have to feed her, I have to feed him. So likewise, pure devotees are dependent on the mother, completely dependent on the Lord, completely surrendered to the Lord. And as a result, the Lord also reciprocates. The devotees try to serve the Lord and the Lord in turn tries to serve the devotees. That is our constitutional position. Sometimes we become little jealous that why we should serve the Lord. We get a picture as if Lord is some kind of a cruel master, who is demanding, just like in case of slaves. He is demanding some kind of service from us. Better you serve me, otherwise you are doomed. That is not the scenario. In the spiritual world, the devotees are trying their best to serve the Lord, and the Lord in turn is making plans how to serve the devotees, which is exemplified in the case of Arjuna. Arjuna was asked, what do you want? You want me or you want my army? The condition being that I will not lift the weapons, I will not fight. Because Arjuna knew the position of the Lord, it was a no-brainer for Arjuna, he chose, I want you, my Lord. And because Arjuna chose Krishna, he was completely dependent on the Lord, he knew that if the Lord is with me, I don't have to worry about anything. In fact, as we all know, Arjuna had Krishna by his side and he eventually won the battle. Although in terms of army, in terms of number of soldiers, in terms of the generals, Duryodhana and the Kauravas were far more powerful. In fact, Arjuna was given, you want me or you want my army? Arjuna chose Krishna, knowing very well that if I have Krishna, I have everything. But Duryodhana, apparently, materially speaking, was wise, and he said, what's the use of Krishna? Let me have the army. So, because Arjuna chose Krishna, he was completely dependent on the Lord, surrendered to the Lord, Krishna reciprocated, literally in the sense that he served Arjuna in the battle of Kurukshetra as Parthasarathy, as the chariot driver. And we all know at all crucial times, critical times, Krishna intervened

Audience: Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
 
Content: After the battle, when Arjuna was assigned the task of taking the queens of Dwaraka, he was attacked by some dacoits. And these dacoits were not some powerful warriors, armed with all kinds of emanations and weapons. They were having some sticks in their hand, like cowards' men. And they were attacked. And Arjuna did try to protect the queens. But unfortunately, they overpowered them. And at that point, after having got defeated by these so-called dacoits, Arjuna, there's a verse which says that, Arjuna is saying, O Lord, this is the same Arjuna, the same Arjuna who became victorious in the battle of Kurukshetra. This is the same Gandiva bow. This is the same chariot. The same chariot in which Arjuna was seated in the battle of Kurukshetra. This is the same chariot driven by the same horses. In other words, all the circumstances were the same in terms of his abilities, his strength, the chariot, the horses. But he says, without you, I am helpless. I couldn't do much. So that is the position of the devotee. In fact, this is the reason why a devotee is not proud. Even if he accomplishes something big, he knows that it is all because of the mercy of the Lord. He knows that it is not artificially psyching himself or sentimentally believing that, no, no, no, sometimes people try to fake some humility. No, it is not because of me, it is because of X, it could be because of something else. They try to pass on the credit to their fellowmen, to their colleagues, to their teammates. But Arjuna was very clear, being conscious of the Lord. He said, it is because of you. So when a devotee is completely surrendered to the Lord, the Lord also accordingly serves his devotees, intervenes. In case of materialists or people who are not at all surrendered to the Lord, who are not at all conscious of the Lord, Krishna is the witness and sanctioner. This law of karma is acting impersonally, just like law of gravity. If I throw a ball, if anybody else throws a ball, it follows a law, the law of gravity, it acts impersonally. So law of karma also acts impersonally, in case we are not surrendered to the Lord. But when we are surrendered to the Lord, then the Lord is not just the witnesser and sanctioner, He is also the director. He takes charge of the life of devotees.
Themes: free will, surrender, divine intervention
 
URL: https://youtu.be/GccuoDdnBGw?t=875
 
Content-type: philosophy
 
Audience: Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
 
Content: Just like in case of, you know, when the bridge was being built to Lanka, as per the law, the stone should be sinking in the water. But the stones were floating. So free will, we have the free will, the Lord does not intervene in terms of how we exercise our free will. The choice is ours. If that free will is not given, then there is no difference between us and, let's say, a programmable machine, a robot, or for that matter, a computer. We have a software, we execute that code and this computer gives the output. So there is no difference. But we are not like robots. Krishna has given us a choice. If we voluntarily choose to serve the Lord, to think of the Lord, to depend on the Lord, to come back to our original constitutional position, the Lord reciprocates. So if we have to experience the intervention of the Lord in our lives, it cannot be just by praying to the Lord in the times of difficulty. There is a quote which is often floated around. It says that if you are praying, only when you are in problem, you have a problem. In other words, we cannot have Lord as, just like when we have something broken, we take out quick fix, fevicol, and we apply it, and once the work is done, the joint is made, we put it back in the drawer. So we cannot use Lord as a quick fix to quick fix our problems. At times we are disappointed that the Lord is not intervening. So this relationship with the Lord, the surrender to the Lord, is a process which we have to follow.
Themes: devotion, consciousness, obstacles

Content-type: philosophy
 
Audience: Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
 
Content: It's a path which we have to undertake. And all the injunctions which is given in the Gita is to inspire us, to motivate us, to tread that path, to depend on the Lord, to think of the Lord, to become his devotee. Krishna in the Gita says, mak chitta sarva durgaani mat prasadat tarishas If you become conscious of me, although he does not force, he says, if you become conscious of me, he could have forced us to become conscious of him, but he doesn't do that. mak chitta sarva durgaani mat prasadat tarishasi If you become conscious of me by my grace, how do we achieve God's grace, Lord's intervention? If we think of the Lord, if we are conscious of the Lord. mak chitta sarva durgaani mat prasadat tarishasi atha chettu ahankaram But if you act out of false ego, not remembering the Lord, in our scenario, most of the time what so happens is we are completely forgetful of the Lord and we remember the Lord only at times of difficulty. Whereas Lord is saying, if you become conscious of me by my grace, you will cross over all obstacles of conditional life.

Content-type: story
 
Audience: Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
 
Content: So, to give an example of, I think we covered last last session, Draupadi. As we all know, Dushashan and the Kauravas in the gambling match, the Pandavas lost. They even at one point put on bet Draupadi and she was lost. And Dushashan unfortunately in the whole assembly attempted, because now the Draupadi was belonging to them. They had all rights on her. When you lose something, it goes to the other person. So, Dushashan attempted to strip Draupadi in the whole assembly. And Draupadi appealed, begged. Looked for help all around. Just like anybody in danger, we look for help. More so women in this kind of a scenario. She looked at her husbands, the five Pandavas imploringly as if, please come forward, save me, protect me. But since they had lost the match and lost her, their heads were down in shame. They couldn't come forward. Then she looked at Bhishma Pitamah, who is the Grandshire of the Kuru dynasty. She even tried to implore Bhishma, please come forward and step and save me. But Bhishma also was helpless. Then finally she knew that if she has to save, she has to depend on her whole strength. And then she caught hold of the saree with both hands very tightly. And on the other side, there was Dushashan trying to disrobe her. And she was holding on to the saree very very tightly with all her strength, with all her might, with all her ability, determined that she will not let Dushashan do what he is attempting. But compared to her strength, he was very very powerful. Physically well built, very strong, part of Duryodhan's army, one of the generals. So Draupadi very soon realized that her strength compared to Dushashan's strength is limited. So she begged the Lord. She caught the saree with one hand and with other hand she raised her hands. Surrender means you raise the hands. Just like we have seen sometimes in wars, the soldiers surrender when they are defeated. Surrender means to give oneself to the control of another person. So still she had one hand holding the saree, the other hand she raised. And tried to chant the names of Govinda, calling on Govinda to help her. To help her in that situation. And at that point she did not experience any of Lord's intervention. Maybe the Lord was waiting for her to surrender fully. Lord has wonderful ways to make the devotees experience his reciprocation by forcing the devotees into circumstances where they are left with no choice but to surrender. There is a very wonderful interaction, beautiful interaction between devotees and the Lord. So then Draupadi realizing that she is helpless and she cannot protect herself, raised both hands, let loose her saree. Stopped holding on and raised both hands and said Govinda, Govinda. Completely dependent on the Lord. And at that point the Lord intervened and supplied unlimited length of saree. And on one side Duryodhana, Dushashana was trying to disrobe and there was no end to the saree. And he could not do that. So as you surrender so shall I reciprocate.
Themes: surrender, divine intervention, practical application

Although we can't quantify the reciprocation and surrender like that but we will experience some reciprocation. The very fact that we are continuing in our journey in Krishna Consciousness, the very fact that we are chanting, we are experiencing some reciprocation from the Lord. And in fact towards the end of the Gita Krishna is out of compassion, knowing what is the best self-interest is saying surrender to me. We all have created our own so-called responsibilities, duties. A devotee knows a doctor may have saved his life but the doctor is just a mere instrument in the hand of the Lord. Sarva Karana Karanam is the Lord. So credit goes to the Lord. Due credit goes to the doctor. Due credit goes to the medicine. We are not fanatical. Yes, doctors have a role to play. Parents have a role to play in our upbringing. We give due credit. We give due respect. Knowing very well that our Supreme Lord, He is the cause of all causes.

So, the researchers found out that when it comes to height, weight, it follows, if you were to plot it in a graph, then the graph would look like a bell curve, which is also called the normal distribution. You take average height of people in a particular class, and then some students will be above that average height, and as we move, it tapers down, and then some students will, the height will be much lesser than the average height, and it tapers down on the left side. And if you were to plot, you will get a bell curve, be it height, be it weight, very few people, too much underweight, very few people overweight, average weight, and then it's a bell curve.
Themes: time, effort, results, distribution
 
URL: https://youtu.be/0WMPy9sWEMs?t=1432
 
Content-type: philosophy
 
Audience: Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
 
Content: So, when it comes to time which people spend in working, the time which people invest in their jobs, again, it follows the bell curve. There are few people who work 12 hours, 14 hours, 16 hours, 18 hours a day, there are people like that, workaholics. So, that's one side of the bell curve, and there are people who take it very easy, work for one or two hours in a day. So, as far as the effort being put is also, if you were to plot that, you will get a bell curve. But this research has found out that when it comes to the results, the amount of time what you're spending, and the outcome of, the results of that work, something very unusual is happening, and it does not follow the normal distribution. A person may work for 2 hours, and he may be billionaire, and a person may be working 16 hours, and he may not even have food to eat for his family members.

Content: And earlier times people were, had sharp, good memory, they would hear and they could assimilate that knowledge and also could pass on. So this is called Shruti. Vedavyasa was the literary incarnation of the Lord, put those understanding of Vedas into writing and subdivided into four parts, where we have Rig Veda, Sam Veda, Yajur Veda and Atharva Veda. So all these Vedic literatures, Ramayana, Mahabharata, all these are compiled, they are not authored, mind you. Vedavyasa is not the author. He compiled all the Vedic literatures. And Krishna says. All the sages, I am Vedavyasa. Ushana was the spiritual master of the demons. He was extremely intelligent, farseeing, political and spiritual in every way. And Krishna says, I am Ushana.

The underlying principles are the same, the details may vary from religion to religion. However, god gives his message directly or through his representatives according to time, place, circumstances and the capacity of individuals to understand the message. Just like in an elementary school we have a teacher teaching maths, 2 plus 2 is 4, 2 minus 1 is 1, 3 minus 2 is 1. If somebody asks the teacher what is 2 minus 5, the teacher will say it is not possible to subtract 5 from 2. The same teacher in high school will say it is minus 3. And the same teacher when he teaches students in the higher levels of college, may be teach some algebra, trigonometry and higher advanced maths. So, teacher instructs according to level of understanding of the student. Likewise, different religions teach the same principles, but according to time, places and circumstances.

So the main thing is the Atma, the soul, without which when that Atma leaves the body, we cremate the body or bury the body. Body minus Atma is a dead body. So this Atma is encaged, is entrapped, entrapped in a material body, made of five elements, earth, air, water, fire and ether, and in one sense, trapped in this body. Although the Atma is eternal, never dying, because it is trapped in the body and identified with the body, it experiences death, old age, diseases and rebirth. So the cage what we are talking about is getting trapped in body life after life, punar api janma, punar api marana, much against our wish, in different species of life, as plants, as birds, as aquatics, reptiles, animals, and after many many lifetimes, human beings, which also is not a permanent life form. And this Atma is transmigrating. We all don't want death, but we are forced to experience death, forced to experience old age. Much against our wish, we are forced to experience diseases of various kinds, and nobody is an exception. So today the session is all about what is this cage in which the Atma is trapped, how it is trapped and how we can escape from this cage, this entrapment. So before we get into the details, let's start the session with prayers as usual.

And just when the battle is about to begin, Arjuna requests Krishna, "Krishna, can you please draw the chariot and place it in between the two armies so that I can have a closer look of my opponents, my enemy with whom we are going to fight." So Arjuna, based on the request of Arjuna, Krishna drew the chariot and placed it in between the two armies. And when Arjuna saw the opponents, the enemy against whom he has to fight, it's his own kinsmen, own brothers, own relatives, near and dear ones, Bhishma Pitamah was there, Dronacharya was there, his teacher, spiritual master, Karna, his cousins, his brothers, although they were willing to fight against him, but still they were relatives, they were his own kinsmen. So when Arjuna saw them, he was completely bewildered, and was into a very confused state of mind. The dilemma he had was, should he really fight this battle? Because if he fights this battle, even if he wins the battle, still he won't be able to enjoy the kingdom because it will be tainted with the blood of his own relatives.

But nevertheless let me tell you how this book became popular. Stephen Covey, he along with his wife went on a sabbatical and stayed next to a sea beach for two years and his objective was to introspect, to ruminate, to study and to also figure out what he did. Along with him, he took 200 years of success literature. You know, self-help books, how to win happy, good friends, how to influence people and this thing and so on. So many, Think and Grow Rich, Napoleon Hill, so many bestsellers who have been there. The last 200 years, all the books he took along with him and two years it was all about reading, digesting, introspecting, walking on the beach, trying to understand what are the common underlying principles in all the books. So in that sense, it was not just, you know, study something somewhere, little bit apply mind. So it was a lot of deep introspection, trying to figure out what is the underlying common thread in all these books and that's how this book came out, Seven Habits of Effective People. And one of the habit, the very fact that it is mentioned as one of the habit means that this is something which is common in all the self-help books which is a very important principle and that is seek first to understand then be understood.

Themes: illusion, fear, dreams
 
URL: https://youtu.be/wp2jExojDDU?t=276
 
Content-type: story
 
Audience: Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
 
Content: Once there were two friends staying together. In fact, they decided to stay overnight together. And while one of them was sleeping, the other one noticed that he is blurting out, "Save me, save me," somewhat screaming, maybe he is having a fearful dream. "Save me, save me, tiger is chasing, save me, save me," he is screaming. Mind you, when we dream, although the dream may be unreal, but the experience is real. That's why at times we even perspire. We wake up and then we feel a sense of relief that after all it was a dream. The experience is real. So this person was screaming, the tiger is chasing me, help me, help me. He was trying to run fast in his dream. And this friend of his was standing next to him, next to his bed, watching him screaming because the tiger was chasing him in the dream. Now obviously when you are a friend of someone and someone is in danger, naturally you will like to pitch in to help that person. So now this person was in a dilemma. What should he do to help this friend? To help this friend of his who was being chased by a tiger in his dream? The two options were, one, he can take a stick or arm himself with some kind of a gun or a pistol to kill the tiger and get into the dream world of his friend with that gun, with that stick, kill the tiger, and save his friend so that his friend can then relax and feel peaceful. That is option one. Option two, he wakes that friend of his and the moment the friend wakes up, he realizes that it was a dream and all of a sudden without no extraneous effort, all the fear, all the horror, all the stress what he was going through completely goes off. Obviously the first option is not possible. He cannot get into the dream world of a person with a stick and try to kill the tiger to give him the relief. The only option is he wakes up that person knowing that he is having a dream, and once the person wakes up, there is no tiger, there is no fear.

Our scriptures also say, just like we have a dream in the night, and then we wake up, the dream is gone. Dream is for two hours, three hours, four hours, at best maybe for the length of the night. And when we wake up, we realize that it was a dream, it was unreal. We went into a forest, we were being chased by a tiger, it was unreal. Or something very inauspicious happened to us, it was unreal. When we wake up, all that inauspiciousness is gone. So from the perspective of the soul, even this life is like a dream, because this is also temporary, and when we leave this body, we take up another body, just like when we come out of the dream world, we get into another world. So this life, you can call it as an extended dream or a daydream. Because from the perspective of soul, this is not our eternal life. We are not eternally Indian, we are not eternally men, we are not eternally, let's say, into a particular business. It's like a dream world. Now in the dream world, just like in a dream world, you can have an inauspicious dream, a horrid dream in which we could experience a lot of fear.

It is free to move around the pole in a radius of 3 meters. It is free to move around the pole in a radius of 3 meters. So in other words the cow is bound to the pole with a rope and the length of the rope is 3 meters, that is the freedom which the cow has. Now imagine a shorter rope, let's say the length of the rope is 1 meter only, the cow still has freedom, the cow still can move but it can move in a radius of 1 meter only, the freedom is relatively compared to the earlier case restricted. It is not as free as the case where the cow has the rope which is 3 meters long. Now imagine another case where the cow is tied so tightly to the pole that the length of the rope is practically zero. Guna also means ropes, just like ropes you have seen, ropes are made of strands which are intertwined, there are three strands which are generally intertwined, it can be thicker rope, many more strands all intertwined. Likewise this rope which binds us to this material world, the three gunas, that rope is made of three strands and the three strands are the ropes. So if the rope is made of three strands then we can say that the length of the rope is little longer.

Themes: spiritual evolution, Satvikahar, Nafi delicacy
 
URL: https://youtu.be/qAgYUIXrGTc?t=1314
 
Content-type: story
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: But there are people who are having taste for that because they are in the influence of such modes that they are drawn towards Tamasikahar, drawn towards Rajasikahar. So, this change happens with everyone who is practicing spiritual principles seriously. Srila Prabhupada once went to Burma. And in Burma, Srila Prabhupada was staying in somebody's place, some devotee's place. And, we have a concept of festivals, kirtans, inviting lot of people and then having a feast, love feast. In Burma, there is a delicacy called Nafi. What they do is, Burmese people, when they get non-vegetarian foodstuffs into their homes, like fish, eggs, or chicken, there are some parts of that meat which cannot be consumed or eaten. I don't want to get into details. So, what they do is, they take those parts and put it in a jar, keep the jar in one side of the house, and let that decompose. And, over many days, in fact, over many months, it decomposes and ferments, just like the grapes ferment and becomes wine. So, they ferment, they decompose, and a liquid starts oozing out from that, so called, refuse of the meat, which cannot be otherwise consumed. And, then they strain that liquid, and especially on festival days, or on some special occasion, that liquid is also served along with the food, and that's a delicacy. That's called Nafi.

URL: https://youtu.be/Zq9aZj8OV-I?t=1032

Content: Many people are not aware of this understanding of sleep cycle. When we go to rest in the night, when we sleep, we go through different stages of sleep. It is not that we just go to sleep and then there is a constant, we have deep sleep and then morning when the alarm rings or when we want to wake up, we wake up. There are different stages of sleep and as I said, many people have done research on this. So they have figured out. There are different ways of measuring the brain waves when we are sleeping, just like heartbeat can be measured, heart rate can be measured. So when we go to bed, we go through a stage which is between wakeful stage and deep sleep stage. That is called stage one, where the number of cycles per second, a brain frequency at which the brain, the neurons vibrate, reduces. And then we get into stage two sleep, which is between stage one and stage three which is deep sleep stage. Deep sleep stage or stage three is a time when actually all the growth hormones, all the repairing work. sending signals to various parts of the body, getting signals back from the various parts, because ultimately assuming that a person is, you know, some things have to be repaired, he has got a cut in his body, signals have to go back and forth. Just like I was thinking, just like when airplane comes back and lands, it has to go to a hangar for some repairs and that is a time when all the, you know, the engineers work on the plane, figure out where it has gone wrong, what parts have gone faulty, removing those parts, replacing those parts and then the plane comes back out of the hangar and then it can be used for, you know, for its normal operations. So, then we have deep sleep which is called stage three and this is where actually the stage three is the most important part where the body gets rejuvenated.

Content: The previous verse Krishna says, 'Aham sarvasya prabhavo matta sarvam pravartate.' We covered it last week. I am the source of everything, material and spiritual. Everything emanates from me. Anyone remembers the next two lines? 'Aham sarvasya prabhavo matta sarvam pravartate. Iti matva bhajante maam.' 'Iti matva' means, with this understanding, 'matva' means understanding. 'Bhajante maam,' worship me, glorify me. 'Budha bhava samanvita,' budha means one who is intelligent, one who is wise, one who has realized this understanding, who has come to this conclusion. Budha bhava, bhava means with feelings, with emotions, with love, with devotion. Samanvita means along with, together with. Having understood that Krishna is the absolute truth, the cause of all causes, everything emanates from him, a person who is wise, who comes to this understanding, he worships me with bhava, with love, with devotion.

All the great sages like Narada, Asita, Devala, Vyasa, proclaim this of you and now you yourself are declaring it to me.

The supreme Lord, many times people say, is energy. Right? This understanding is not wrong, but it is not the complete understanding. Just like you have the sun. You have the sun god, you have sun planet and you have sunshine. When sunshine enters the house, we say sun has come. In one sense it is not wrong, because sunshine is energy of sun, emanating from sun. Sunshine has no place without sun. There cannot be sunshine without sun. So, Brahman is impersonal, all pervading aspect. Brahman is impersonal, all pervading aspect. The aspect of the Supreme Lord.

When I touch fire, then I become, I feel the heat of fire. When I put an iron rod in fire, a time comes when the iron rod becomes red hot, almost becomes like fire. So likewise, when I associate with the Lord, specifically in this age, by chanting the holy names of the Lord, I contact, I touch, I associate with the Lord who is all-pure, who is reservoir of sat, chit and ananda. When there was nothing, He was there. And everything is emanating from Him. We can have that perfect knowledge and faith and that understanding only by the grace of Supreme Lord.

Content: That's why Krishna says it is Rajvidya Rajguhyam. By analytical understanding, we cannot come to this conclusion. Once there was a Brahmin, who was engaged in different kinds of spiritual activities, rituals, penances, austerities. And he happened to meet Narada, who was on his way to Vaikuntha. How many of you have heard this story? Ok, briefly I will share. So, this Brahmin was striving for salvation, liberation. So he knew that Narada is very close to Lord Vishnu, Narayana. He keeps going to Vaikuntha. So he said, when you meet Narayana, you ask him this question, this Brahmin, he is striving hard for mukti or liberation. When will he get his mukti? Narada said, okay, I will ask.
Themes: Narada's Journey, Questions about Liberation, The Lord's Response
 
URL: https://youtu.be/goTACIX3zuw?t=757
 
Content-type: story
 
Audience: Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
 
Content: On his way to Vaikuntha, he also happened to meet a cobbler. The cobbler also asked the same question. If you happen to meet Narayana, please ask him, when will I get my liberation? So Narada Muni went to Vaikuntha. He happened to meet Narayana after meeting him, after paying obeisances, after all that interaction. He asked this question. I met this Brahmin who is engaged in lot of austerities and penances. He has requested me to ask this question, when is he going to get his liberation? So the Lord said, he may have to wait for many, many lifetimes. So he was surprised. Then he asked, by the way, I met the cobbler also. He also asked the same question, when is he going to get his liberation? So he said, after he finishes this life, he is going to come back to me. So Narada was surprised. The cobbler, day in and day out, he is working with leather, leather shoes and all those kind of, not a very quote unquote, a glorious profession. He is going to come the very next life and Brahmin who is chanting mantras, sacrifices, rituals, is going to wait for many, many lifetimes. So he said, I am not able to fathom, I am not able to understand, why so? So he says, when you go back and in case they inquire what the Lord was doing, just tell them, one thing. If they ask what the Lord was doing in Vaikuntha, tell them that the Lord was trying to pass an elephant through a needle, the eye of a needle. So Narada said, okay.
Themes: Faith vs. Doubt, Conviction in the Lord, The Cobbler's Realization
 
URL: https://youtu.be/goTACIX3zuw?t=868
 
Content-type: story
 
Audience: Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
 
Content: So first he went to Brahmana. So he, first question, do you meet Narayana? Yes, yes, I met him. What was Narayana doing there? So he was saying, he was trying to pass an elephant through an eye of a needle. The Brahmana said, how is that possible? What an absurd thing. How can elephant pass through an eye of a needle? No, no, but the Lord was trying to do that. No, no, come on, don't joke. How is it possible? So Narada said, okay. That is his conviction about the Lord. Then he went to the cobbler. The cobbler asked the same question, what was Narayana doing? He said, he was trying to pass an elephant through an eye of a needle. Such a wonderful Lord. How glorious he is. Anything and everything is possible by him. So Narada said, wait a minute. Is it really possible? Do you think the Lord can pass an elephant through an eye of a needle? Is it really possible? He said, why not? You see, I am sitting underneath this banyan tree and it has so many fruits. All of them are falling. Each of them has hundreds of seeds and each seed, has the potency to grow into a full banyan tree. If the Lord can do that, each and every seed has the potential to grow into a whole big banyan tree. I don't think it's difficult for the Lord to pass an elephant through an eye of a needle. My Lord is so great. My Lord is so wonderful. So Narada understood what the Lord meant when he said that he'll come back. So the main important thing is the conviction and faith. And that comes by the blessing and mercy of the Lord.
Themes: Jnana vs. Vijnana, Realization and Faith, Arjuna's Nishtha
 
URL: https://youtu.be/goTACIX3zuw?t=976
 
Content-type: philosophy
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: We can be a big pandit. We could know, verbatim the entire Bhagavad Gita. We can quote all the shlokas. But deep within that conviction, that faith, that the Lord is the supreme controller, He is the cause of all causes, may not be there. Jnana and another thing is called Vijnana. Vijnana means realization. We can have theoretical knowledge but that conviction, that faith, that nishtha, we are blessed with. And that we have to pay a price for that nishtha. And that price, what that price is, we'll discuss as we move forward. So Arjuna had that nishtha. He's saying, yes Krishna, I agree. You are supreme Brahman. You are the supreme pure. You are the absolute truth. I agree with that.

Themes: Parampara System, Disciplic Succession, Distortion of Knowledge
 
URL: https://youtu.be/goTACIX3zuw?t=1443
 
Content-type: philosophy
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: Bhagavad Gita should be understood in a parampara system. And that message, in truth, In Tattvataha we get when we understand Gita in a parampara system. What is parampara? Parampara is the knowledge is spoken by the Lord. It is delivered as it is to the next person in the chain of disciplic succession, to the next person, to the next person, like that. Just like a letter changes many hands. You put in the post box, it goes to the post office, it gets sorted. Next hand, next hand, next hand. Finally it gets delivered to the person who is the recipient, but it is unchanged. That's why Krishna says, I spoke this knowledge to sun god Vivasvan. Vivasvan spoke to Manu. Manu spoke to Ikshwaku. It was coming in a parampara. In due course of time, kala means time. It got, the original intent, the original message, it got distorted. That's why he says, The same knowledge what he spoke to sun god Vivasvan, who spoke to Manu, Manu spoke to Ikshwaku, was coming in a parampara. But because as it is, it was lost, it got distorted.

Bandhan, you are right, bandhan, a person becomes entangled. Bhaya, fear, a person is working hard but there is an insecurity, there is anxiety. And Ma, Ma stands for Mriti. This Per-Per-Bhar-Bharma characterizes our life. We have intense hard work, Parishrama, then we have Phena, we froth, anxiety, stress, hypertension, blood pressure. And then we have Bh, Bandhan, entangled. That very activity which is meant to give us pleasure, satisfy us, entangles us. Bhaya, always fearful. And Ma, we lead that life and that ends in Ma, Mriti. So what is Dharma? Dharma hi apavargasya, that which negates this materialistic life of Pavarga.

Shubha and Ashubha, auspiciousness and inauspiciousness. When people go for a walk, suddenly they find the cat passing the way, they stop. It is considered as inauspicious. Recently there was Dhanteras and I was told that in Jaipur there is one temple where lines and lies of people lined up with new cars, wanting to get the prayers done so that they can have auspicious beginning with the new car. So everyone is concerned about Shubha and Ashubha. Our scriptures give us a very nice perspective of what is auspicious and what is inauspicious. In one sense, the so-called inauspiciousness, there is a lot of auspiciousness in this world and so-called inauspiciousness. Shubha and Ashubha, our scriptures say, it is in one sense Manodharma. It is a mental concoction. We brandish something as Shubha and we label something as Ashubha. But according to scriptures, Shubha, the most auspicious is that activity which brings us closer to Krishna or to God, that activity which makes us remember God. And what is Ashubha? What is inauspiciousness? That activity which takes us away from God, which makes us forget God. Even the so-called pious activity, if it makes us forget God or takes us away from God, it's inauspicious, but it is Ashubha. So with this definition in mind, with this understanding in mind, with this perspective, the best way to start our day is engaging in spiritual activities, which takes us closer to Krishna.

URL: https://youtu.be/8PrjspdH0Sw?t=0
 
Content-type: philosophy
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
 
Content: Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto No. 6, Chapter No. 10, Text No. 9: Etavana vyayodharmaha kunya shloke rupashitaha yo bhuta shoka har shabyam atma shochati hrishyati. Translation: If one is unhappy to see the distress of other living beings and happy to see their happiness, his religious principles are appreciated as imperishable by exalted persons who are considered pious and benevolent. One generally follows different types of religious principles or performs various occupational duties according to the body given to him by the modes of material nature. In these words, however, real religious principles are explained. Everyone should be unhappy to see others in distress and happy to see others happy. Atmavatsarva bhuteshu. One should feel the happiness and distress of others as his own. It is on this basis that the Buddhist religious principles of non-violence, ahimsa bhavata, and paramo dharma is established. We feel pain when someone disturbs us and therefore we should not inflict pain upon other living beings. Lord Buddha's mission was to stop unnecessary animal killing and therefore he preached that the great religious principle is non-violence. One cannot continue killing animals at the same time be a religious man. That is the greatest hypocrisy. Jesus Christ said, 'Do not kill,' but hypocrites nevertheless kill. Hypocrites nevertheless maintain thousands of slaughterhouses while posing as Christians. Such hypocrisy is condemned in this verse. One should be happy to see others happy and one should be unhappy to see others unhappy. This is the principle to be followed.
Themes: compassion, equality, Krishna consciousness

Regardless of whether human, animal, tree or plant, all living entities are the son of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Lord Shri Krishna says in the Bhagavad Gita 14.4: sarva yoni shukontaya murtaya sambhavantiya tasam brahma mahad yonir aham bija pradapitah. It should be understood that all species of life, O son of Kunti, are made possible by birth in this material nature and that I am the seed-giving father. The different forms of these living entities are only their external dresses. Every living being is actually a spirit soul, a part and parcel of God. Therefore, one should not favor only one kind of living being. A Vaishnava sees all living entities as part and parcel of God. As the Lord says in the Bhagavad Gita, all living beings are meant to be a part and parcel of God. He is not a separate entity of the divine. He is the integral unit of the divine. He is the one who describes everything with the divine. So this way, one who is transcendentally situated at one realizes the Supreme Brahman and becomes fully joyful. He never laments nor desires to have anything. He is equally disposed to every living entity. In that state, he attains pure devotional service unto me.
Themes: Vaishnava traits, community, spiritual advancement

Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta Bhava. We were on a long drive and generally we see lorries, big big trucks on highways. And invariably on those lorries, trucks, something is written. Some catchy sentences. Buri najar wale tera mu kaala. I remember having seen one very nice sentence. It struck me. And this is in line with what this verse is saying. It was written in Hindi. URL: https://youtu.be/c_E1Fg1TnOs?t=264
 
Content-type: philosophy
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: So, maya has two forms. All of us have heard about this word maya? Yes, no? Yes. At least you must have heard that maya has two forms. When the light falls on a mirror, it gets reflected. So, one side of the mirror where the light falls and it gets reflected, it is illuminated. It has light. And that place is called Abhasa. Abhasa means that potency of the Lord by which everything can be seen in relation with the Lord, can be perceived. We can perceive the presence of Lord in everything and in everyone. That potency is called yoga-maiya. There is another potency called Mahamaya which is Tamaha. Tamaha means Darkness. The below bottom side of the mirror in which the light falls and gets reflected is Dark. And what is the darkness when we are not able to perceive the Lord, when we see things independent of Lord, when Lord is not in the picture? So devotees of the Lord are under the influence of Yogamaya. Yashoda Mai could not perceive that Krishna is the Supreme Personality of Godhead. She was covered by the Yogamaya potency of the Lord. We who have forgotten the Lord, rebelled against the Lord, are under the influence of Mahamaya, which is darkness.

Maya Haya Andhakaar
. Maya is like darkness. Wherever there is sun, there cannot be any darkness. So likewise, when we bring the Lord into our life, the remembrance of the Lord in our life, when we are able to see things in relationship with the Supreme Lord, Maya has no grip on us. Why we say we are trapped in the web of Maya, because we are doing activities completely oblivious of the Lord and His pleasure, His satisfaction. The goal of life has become my pleasure, my satisfaction. Could be extended pleasure, my family's pleasure, my society's pleasure, my country's pleasure. Lord is out of picture. So that is Tamaha, that is darkness. And that is why it says 
Tamaseema Jyotirgamaya
. Come to light. It is said 
Brahma Satyam Jagat Mithya
.

But perception of rope as snake is Mithya. So when we perceive Jagat, independent of the Lord, with no relation with the Lord, that is Mithya.

Content: tasya putrati tejasvi, niharan balako arba kahe, rajnagam prapitam tatam, sutva tatredam abhravesh. Translation and purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada. Translation: The sage had a son who was very powerful, being a brahmana's son. While he was playing with the inexperienced boys, he heard of his father's distress, which was occasioned by the king. Then and there the boy spoke as follows. Due to Maharaj Pariksit's good government, even a boy of tender age who was playing with his other inexperienced boys could become as powerful as a qualified brahmana. The boy was known as Shringi, and he achieved good training in brahmacharya by his father, so that he could be as powerful as a brahmana, even at that age. But because the age of Kali was seeking an opportunity to spoil the cultural heritage of the four orders of life, the inexperienced boys gave a chance for the age of Kali to enter into the field of Vedic culture. Hatred of the lower orders of life began from the brahmana boy under the influence of Kali, and thus cultural life began to dwindle day after day. The first victim of brahmana injustice, brahmanical injustice, was Maharaj Pariksit, and thus the protection given by the king against the onslaught of Kali was slackened. Thus ends the bhaktivedanta purva.

I came across this story, in fact it's not a story, I saw a television episode more than 20 years back, and those days I think it was black and white television and I even till date remember this. But those days it was not like you like some episode you save it, you can download it, it's on YouTube. I vaguely remember there was a man, he was very, very poverty-stricken and practically had no money with him. He had a young daughter and he was barely able to manage the day-to-day needs of the family, including groceries and clothes. So the daughter was getting to marriageable age and he was in anxiety how to marry the daughter. Even in Vedic times there was, you know, marriage, it was done in a way some dowry was also given, not in the sense what we see in present times it is demanded, it was out of love. So this person was a lot of a lot of anxiety, a lot of worried how to manage his household expenses, how to marry the daughter, and he was trying various means to earn money, but things were not happening. So one day a friend of his gave him a challenge. He said, you need money? I can give you enough money, so much money that for the rest of the life you can live happily. You don't have to worry about your finances. Not only that, you can very easily get your daughter married. So he couldn't believe such a thing as possible. He said, but there is a challenge. Now he was so desperate for money, he said, I am ready to accept any challenge. He said, the challenge is that you have to live in a room all by yourself, all alone, for ten years. Three times the food will be given in that cell, in that room. But you will not be allowed to interact with anyone, including your family, including your near and dear ones. Even in prisons they have, you know, in a month you can, for some five minutes or something you can meet. There are some protocols. But here the condition was that for ten years you cannot talk to anyone, you cannot meet anyone, you cannot hear anything. There were no smartphones, there was no mp3 players. You can hear the songs, FM radio, nothing. And you all know that man is a social animal. We thrive on relationships, social beings. Relationships are very important. So this man accepted the challenge and he thought, instead of struggling the whole life, it's a struggle for ten years. I'll somehow manage. But he never anticipated what that struggle would be, how intense that struggle would be. He thought. So this whole documentary or film what was made is basically to showcase his emotions, what this person went through, what struggles. And this whole camera was focusing on his facial expressions, on what he was doing during those ten days. These days you have some reality shows. Very. Somebody is put in some house. I haven't seen anything, just maybe some idea. And then people watch what they are doing. So for ten years he was in this room, locked. Three times food was served to him. And then first initial one week, ten days, he was, he started missing something, something unusual. See, one day we can manage with food, second day, you know, something, we feel very uncomfortable. So likewise, if we are in a room all by itself, one day, two day, we can somehow manage. So after one week, ten days, he started feeling very uneasy and that started showing up on his face, on his expression. He started becoming uncomfortable, little jittery, felt like talking to someone. There's nobody. Even the camera captured whatever he was speaking, whatever he was expressing, whatever words he was speaking also was being captured, was being shown. So he even started thinking that, is it really worthwhile taking this challenge and stay for ten years? Because seven days itself was so difficult. And he started even thinking, maybe, let me walk out. But then, what else will he do? How will he get the money? So he continued. One month passed, two months passed, three months passed. And then, after three months, he became so agitated because of loneliness. No companionship, nobody to talk to. Sometimes he would be shouting, screaming, hitting his head on the walls. He started becoming a little bit mad.

URL: https://youtu.be/RxWcBMs-zpY?t=33
 
Content-type: philosophy
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
 
Content: Translation and purport by His Divine Grace, Sri Bhaktivedanta Swamishila Prabhupāda. Translation: Upon returning, he began to contemplate and argue within himself whether the sage had actually been in meditation with the sense concentrated and eyes closed, or whether he had just been feigning trance just to avoid receiving a lower kshatriya. Purport: A king being a devotee of the Lord did not approve of his own action and thus he began to wonder whether the sage was really in a trance or was just pretending in order to avoid receiving the king. He was a kshatriya and therefore lower in rank. Repentance comes in the mind of a good soul as soon as he commits something wrong. Srila Vishwanatha Chakravarti Thakura and Srila Jiva Goswami do not believe that the king's action was due to his past misdeeds. The arrangements were so made by the Lord just to call the king back to home, back to Godhead. According to Srila Vishwanatha Chakravarti, the plan was made by the will of the Lord and by the will of the Lord the situation of frustration was created. The plan was that for his so-called misdeed the king could be cursed by an inexperienced Brahmana boy infected by the influence of Kali and thus the king would leave his hearth and womb for good. His connection with Srila Shukadeva Goswami would enable the presentation of great Srimad Bhagavatam which is considered to be a book incarnation of the Lord. This book incarnation of the Lord gives much fascinating information of the transcendental pastime of the Lord like his Rasalila with the spiritual cowherd damsels of Rajabhumi. This specific pastime of the Lord has special significance because anyone who properly learns about this particular pastime of the Lord will certainly be desuited from mundane sex desire and be placed on the path of sublime devotional service to the Lord. The pure devotee's mundane frustration is meant to elevate the devotee to a higher transcendental position. By placing Arjuna and Pandavas in frustration due to the interview of their cousin brothers the prelude of battle of Urukshetra was created by the Lord. This was so incarnated the sound representation of the Lord Bhagavad Gita. So by placing Arjuna and Pandavas and by placing King Pariksit in an acquired position the incarnation of Srimad Bhagavatam was created by the will of the Lord. Being distressed by the hunger and thirst was only a show because King endured much even in the womb of his mother. He was never disturbed by the glaring heat of Brahmastra released by Ashwatthama. The King's distressed condition was certainly unprecedented. The devotee like Maharaj Pariksit are powerful enough to forbear such distress. Such distress by the will of the Lord and they are never disturbed. This situation in this case was therefore all planned by the Lord. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta Karupada.

Themes: material provision, sacrifice, Vedic principles
 
URL: https://youtu.be/aGiP3lIMzLc?t=0
 
Content-type: philosophy
 
Audience: Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
 
Content: Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, canto number 4, chapter number 19, text number 9. Translation and purport by His Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Shri Prabhupada. Translation: King Pruthu was presented with various gifts from the general populace and predominating deities of all planets. The ocean and seas were full of valuable jewels and pearls, and the hills were full of chemicals and fertilizers. Four kinds of edibles were produced profusely. As stated in the Isopanishad, this material creation is supplied with all the potencies for the production of all necessities required by the living entities, not only human beings, but animals, reptiles, aquatic species. The ocean and seas produce pearls, coral, and valuable jewels so that fortunate law-abiding people can utilize them. Similarly, the hills are full of chemicals so that when rivers flow down from them, the chemicals spread over the fields to fertilize the four kinds of foodstuff. They are technically known as charviya, those edibles which are chewed, lehiya, those which are licked up, chhusiya, those which are swallowed, and piya, those which are drunk. Pruthu Maharaj was greeted by the residents of other planets and their presiding deities. They presented various gifts to the king and acknowledged him as the proper type of king by whose planning and activities everyone throughout the universe could be happy and prosperous. It is clearly indicated in these words that the oceans and seas are meant for producing jewels, but in Kali Yuga, the oceans are mainly being utilized for fishing. Shudras and poor men were allowed to fish, but the higher classes like the Kshatriyas and Vaishyas would gather pearls, jewels, and coral. All the poor men would catch tons of fish. They would not be equal in value to the piece of coral or pearl. In this age, so many factories for the manufacture of fertilizers have been opened, but when the personality of Godhead is pleased by the performance of yagnas, the hills automatically produce fertilizing chemicals, which help produce edibles in the field. Everything is dependent on Godhead. Everything is dependent on the people's acceptance of the Vedic principle of sacrifice. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta.
Themes: illusion, Maya, detachment
