Content: There was a devotee of Krishna who was in company of the Lord. He was walking with the Lord and then in course of the walk he asked the Lord, Lord Shri Krishna, I have heard about your divine energy Maya. Can you please explain what is this divine energy and how it influences the living entity? Why is it called Dhrittaya? Generally devotees who are especially pure devotees, they are out of the purview of Maya. So the Lord said, well, this is a big subject. In fact, the entire material world is nothing but play of Maya. The living entity wants to enjoy and the whole world, whatever is happening, is the illusory energy of the Lord captivating the living entities. So it's a long story. So anyway, the Lord told that devotee, let's sit and talk whenever we have some long discussion. So we say, okay, let's sit and talk. It's going to take time. So they were, they sat down on one rock and then Krishna, because it's going to be a long story, long description. So Krishna said, before we start, can you give me a, get me a glass of water? So, yes, my Lord. So the devotee immediately went in search for water. It was open land, hot summer day. And then he walked for a while. He could not see anywhere where he could get water. Kept walking, kept walking, kept walking. And then fortunately at a distance, he could see some greenery, some cottages, some kind of a village. So at last he was very happy that for sure here I will get water. And then I'll go back. And then I listened from the Lord. What did he listen? About Maya. So he went to that village and then he saw one cottage from a distance, went to that cottage, knocked on the door and the door opened. That very beautiful, charming lady opened the door. And in earlier times, Vedic times, a guest was considered as an Athito Devobhava. Unlike present times, beware of dogs. Even if there is no dog, they'll put that. Beware, don't come in. So, the moment the door opened, that lady with a folded hand said, Swami, come in. Swami came in, very beautiful, handsome, charming, attractive lady. Come in and sit. She sat down. And then, you know, they all started getting panicky. And, you know, children, grandchildren, and they started saving and, you know, trying to, the cattle, the lamb, whatever there in the home, they were trying to safeguard whatever the grains were kept. And then soon the water kept on rising. And so much of current, they started seeing that many of the things started flowing away with the water. And they were all desperately trying to save themselves, save everything what they had, the valuables. And in front of their eyes, one by one, one by one, everything started flowing with the water. So much so that the more they tried, the more they tried to hold on to things, but right in front of their eyes, everything was getting wiped away. So he said, Krishna, Krishna, please, Krishna, please help. Finally, in front of his eyes, everything got swept away. And then finally, you know, pleading the Lord, please, please save me, save me. He fell at the feet of the Lord. Save me, Govinda. And then he hears a tap. He opens his eyes. Krishna says, where is the glass of water?

Even the service what we do is a Yagna. Even chanting what we do is a Yagna. Yagnair Sankirtana Prayer. So, there was once a devotee who came to Shila Prabhupada. So, many times we have some false understanding that, you know, this service is Deity service or Pujari service or some services which have, which quote unquote we get a feeling that they are very spiritual in nature. And there are some services which are not. Which involves lot of management, dealing with outside people. So, Prabhupada gave us a very broader and a holistic understanding of Krishna Consciousness. He said, in Kali Yuga, for propagating this Krishna Consciousness movement, there will be lot of managerial work. But then he says, Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu promises that, if we are doing this managerial work, we will not be entangled. So, Yagna, which here we are talking about is not just, you know, doing fire Yagna or doing Sankirtana Yagna. It is broader understanding. All different forms of services which we are doing for the pleasure of the Lord is a Yagna. Management also is a Yagna.
